title: The Memory of a Wooden Bench
text_zh: 公园里有一把老木椅，椅面已经被无数人的坐姿磨出了一个浅浅的凹陷。我第一次注意到它是因为那个凹陷的形状异常规则——不是圆形的，而是一个微微偏左的椭圆，说明大多数坐在这里的人都习惯把重心放在左侧。椅背上有几道平行的划痕，间距均匀，像是有人长期把背包挂在椅背上摩擦留下的。扶手的两端磨损程度也不对称：右边的扶手比左边光滑得多，暗示着人们起身时更多地依赖右手撑扶手的力量。木材的纹理在这些磨损处变得格外清晰，就像河床在水流冲刷下暴露出底层的岩石。一个阴天的下午，我坐在这把椅子上待了整整三个小时。期间有六个人先后坐过——一对中年夫妇分享了半块面包，一个年轻女孩看了四十分钟的书，一个老人闭着眼睛晒了一会儿太阳。他们都不知道自己正在为这把椅子添加新的记忆层。木头是有耐心的，它用几十年的时间来记录人类短暂的停留。
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yì bǎ lǎo mù yǐ, yǐ miàn yǐjīng bèi wúshù rén de zuòzī mó chū le yí gè qiǎnqiǎn de āoxiàn. Wǒ dì yī cì zhùyì dào tā shì yīnwèi nàge āoxiàn de xíngzhuàng yìcháng guīzé — bú shì yuánxíng de, ér shì yí gè wēiwēi piān zuǒ de tuǒyuán, shuōmíng dà duōshù zuò zài zhèlǐ de rén dōu xíguàn bǎ zhòngxīn fàng zài zuǒcè. Yǐ bèi shàng yǒu jǐ dào píngxíng de huàhén, jiānjù jūnyún, xiàng shì yǒu rén chángqī bǎ bēibāo guà zài yǐ bèi shàng mócā liú xià de. Fúshǒu de liǎng duān mósǔn chéngdù yě bú duìchèn: yòubiān de fúshǒu bǐ zuǒbiān guānghuá de duō, ànshì zhe rénmen qǐshēn shí gèng duō de yīlài yòushǒu chēng fúshǒu de lìliàng. Mùcái de wénlǐ zài zhèxiē mósǔn chù biàn de géwài qīngxī, jiù xiàng héchuáng zài shuǐliú chōngshuā xià bàolù chū dǐcéng de yánshí. Yí gè yīn tiān de xiàwǔ, wǒ zuò zài zhè bǎ yǐzi shàng dāi le zhěngzhěng sān gè xiǎoshí. Qījiān yǒu liù gè rén xiānhòu zuò guò — yí duì zhōngnián fūfù fēnxiǎng le bàn kuài miànbāo, yí gè niánqīng nǚhái kàn le sìshí fēnzhōng de shū, yí gè lǎorén bì zhe yǎnjīng shài le yíhuìr tàiyáng. Tāmen dōu bù zhīdào zìjǐ zhèngzài wèi zhè bǎ yǐzi tiānjiā xīn de jìyì céng. Mùtou shì yǒu nàixīn de, tā yòng jǐ shí nián de shíjiān lái jìlù rénlèi duǎnzàn de tíngliú.
text_en: There's an old wooden bench in the park whose seat has been worn into a shallow depression by countless people's sitting postures. I first noticed it because the depression was unusually regular in shape — not circular, but a slightly left-leaning oval, suggesting most people who sit here habitually shift their weight to the left side. The backrest bears several parallel scratches, evenly spaced, as if someone long hung a backpack there, leaving friction marks. The wear on the two armrests is also asymmetric: the right armrest is much smoother than the left, hinting that people rely more on their right hand pushing against the armrest when standing up. The wood grain at these worn spots has become exceptionally clear, like a riverbed exposing underlying rock after water erosion. On an overcast afternoon, I sat on this bench for a full three hours. During that time six people sat down in succession — a middle-aged couple shared half a piece of bread, a young girl read for forty minutes, an old man closed his eyes and basked in the sun for a while. None of them knew they were adding new memory layers to this bench. Wood is patient — it uses decades to record humanity's brief pauses.

questions.q_zh: 这段话对'时间'的处理方式说明了什么？
questions.q_en: What does the way this passage handles 'time' suggest?

options.text: 时间不只是客观的流逝
options.pinyin: shíjiān bù zhǐ shì kèguān de liúshì
options.text_en: time is more than objective passing

options.pinyin: xūyào gèng hǎo de guǎnlǐ shíjiān

questions.explanation: The passage converts measurable duration into felt experience — physical traces of time carry emotional weight, making it subjective and layered, not merely chronological.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的写作手法有什么特点？
questions.q_en: What is distinctive about the writing technique in this passage?

options.text: 以小见大，用细节传达深意
options.pinyin: yǐ xiǎo jiàn dà, yòng xìjié chuándá shēnyì
options.text_en: uses small details to convey big meaning

questions.explanation: The passage extracts large thematic meaning from minute physical observations — wear marks, textures, small behaviors — using the particular to illuminate the universal.

title: The Seasons of a Stairwell
text_zh: 我住的这栋楼有六层，没有电梯。每天爬楼梯的过程中，我发现楼梯间其实有自己的四季。春天的时候，二楼张阿姨家的窗户总是开着，楼梯间会飘进一股淡淡的槐花香气，和水泥墙壁的冷硬气息形成奇妙的对比。夏天最为明显：楼梯间变成了一个天然的烟囱效应装置，热空气从底层往上升腾，顶楼的温度比一楼高出至少五度。我曾经在不同楼层放置温度计，绘制了一张纵向的温度分布图。秋天的楼梯间弥漫着各家厨房飘出的炖肉和煮汤的气味，这些气味沿着楼梯盘旋上升，在三楼的拐角处交汇融合，形成一种复杂的嗅觉和声。冬天的楼梯间最安静，水泥台阶上有时会结一层薄霜，脚踩上去发出细碎的「咯吱」声。暖气管道贴着楼梯间的墙壁延伸，偶尔发出金属膨胀的轻响，像是这栋老楼在寒冷中深深地叹了一口气。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù de zhè dòng lóu yǒu liù céng, méiyǒu diàntī. Měi tiān pá lóutī de guòchéng zhōng, wǒ fāxiàn lóutī jiān qíshí yǒu zìjǐ de sìjì. Chūntiān de shíhou, èr lóu Zhāng āyí jiā de chuānghù zǒng shì kāi zhe, lóutī jiān huì piāo jìn yì gǔ dàndàn de huáihuā xiāngqì, hé shuǐní qiángbì de lěng yìng qìxī xíngchéng qímiào de duìbǐ. Xiàtiān zuìwéi míngxiǎn: lóutī jiān biàn chéng le yí gè tiānrán de yāncōng xiàoyìng zhuāngzhì, rè kōngqì cóng dǐcéng wǎng shàng shēngténg, dǐng lóu de wēndù bǐ yī lóu gāo chū zhìshǎo wǔ dù. Wǒ céngjīng zài bùtóng lóucéng fàngzhì wēndùjì, huìzhì le yì zhāng zòngxiàng de wēndù fēnbù tú. Qiūtiān de lóutī jiān mímàn zhe gè jiā chúfáng piāo chū de dùn ròu hé zhǔ tāng de qìwèi, zhèxiē qìwèi yán zhe lóutī pánxuán shàngshēng, zài sān lóu de guǎijiǎo chù jiāohuì rónghé, xíngchéng yì zhǒng fùzá de xiùjué héshēng. Dōngtiān de lóutī jiān zuì ānjìng, shuǐní táijiē shàng yǒushí huì jié yì céng bó shuāng, jiǎo cǎi shàngqù fāchū xìsuì de 「gēzhī」 shēng. Nuǎnqì guǎndào tiē zhe lóutī jiān de qiángbì yánshēn, ǒu'ěr fāchū jīnshǔ péngzhàng de qīng xiǎng, xiàng shì zhè dòng lǎo lóu zài hánlěng zhōng shēnshēn de tàn le yì kǒu qì.
text_en: The building I live in has six floors and no elevator. During my daily stair-climbing, I've discovered that the stairwell actually has its own four seasons. In spring, Auntie Zhang on the second floor always has her window open, and a faint fragrance of locust blossoms drifts into the stairwell, forming a wonderful contrast with the cold, hard scent of concrete walls. Summer is most obvious: the stairwell becomes a natural chimney effect device, with hot air rising from the ground floor, making the top floor at least five degrees warmer than the first. I once placed thermometers on different floors and drew a vertical temperature distribution chart. In autumn, the stairwell fills with the aromas of stewed meat and simmering soup drifting from each family's kitchen — these scents spiral upward along the stairs, converging and blending at the third-floor landing into a complex olfactory chord. The stairwell is quietest in winter; sometimes a thin frost forms on the concrete steps, making a faint crunching sound underfoot. Heating pipes extend along the stairwell walls, occasionally emitting the light sound of metal expansion, as if the old building were sighing deeply in the cold.

questions.q_zh: 夏天楼梯间里发生了什么现象？
questions.q_en: What phenomenon occurs in the stairwell during summer?

options.pinyin: lóutī jiān biàn de fēicháng lěng

options.text: 热空气上升，顶楼比一楼高出五度以上
options.pinyin: rè kōngqì shàngshēng, dǐng lóu bǐ yī lóu gāo chū wǔ dù yǐshàng
options.text_en: Hot air rises, top floor is at least 5 degrees warmer
Every floor has the same temperature

questions.explanation: In summer the stairwell acts as a natural chimney — hot air rises through it, making the top floor at least five degrees warmer than the bottom.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中最关键的转折是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most important turning point in this passage?
questions.explanation: The passage shifts from cataloging seasonal sensory data — scents, temperatures, sounds — to personifying the building as a living entity that sighs, marking a change in interpretive awareness.

title: Mapping Shadows
text_zh: 有一段时间我迷上了观察建筑物的影子。每天下午三点，我会站在同一个路口，记录周围建筑投射在地面上的影子轮廓。一个月以后，我积累了三十张影子地图，叠放在一起就能清楚地看到影子随季节迁移的轨迹。冬天的影子又长又窄，像是建筑物伸出的细长手臂，试图触摸远处的地面。夏天的影子则缩短为建筑物脚下的一小片暗影，仿佛它们不愿意离开自己的身体太远。最让我感到惊讶的是，某些影子区域在一天之中从未被阳光照射过——它们是永恒的阴影地带，潮湿而阴凉，催生了独特的微生态：墙角的苔藓、缝隙中的蕨类植物，甚至有几只只在阴影中活动的猫。我把这些常年不见阳光的区域称为「影子群岛」，它们像散落在城市光明海洋中的暗色小岛。一个晴朗的冬日午后，我发现两栋楼的影子在街道中央短暂地交叉重叠，形成了一个更深的暗影——那是两栋楼唯一的一次「握手」，每天只持续大约十五分钟。
text_pinyin: Yǒu yí duàn shíjiān wǒ mí shàng le guānchá jiànzhùwù de yǐngzi. Měi tiān xiàwǔ sān diǎn, wǒ huì zhàn zài tóng yí gè lùkǒu, jìlù zhōuwéi jiànzhù tóushè zài dìmiàn shàng de yǐngzi lúnkuò. Yí gè yuè yǐhòu, wǒ jīlěi le sānshí zhāng yǐngzi dìtú, dié fàng zài yìqǐ jiù néng qīngchǔ de kàn dào yǐngzi suí jìjié qiānyí de guǐjì. Dōngtiān de yǐngzi yòu cháng yòu zhǎi, xiàng shì jiànzhùwù shēn chū de xìcháng shǒubì, shìtú chùmō yuǎnchù de dìmiàn. Xiàtiān de yǐngzi zé suōduǎn wéi jiànzhùwù jiǎo xià de yì xiǎo piàn ànyǐng, fǎngfú tāmen bú yuànyì líkāi zìjǐ de shēntǐ tài yuǎn. Zuì ràng wǒ gǎndào jīngyà de shì, mǒu xiē yǐngzi qūyù zài yì tiān zhī zhōng cóngwèi bèi yángguāng zhàoshè guò — tāmen shì yǒnghéng de yīnyǐng dìdài, cháoshī ér yīnliáng, cuīshēng le dútè de wēi shēngtài: qiáng jiǎo de táixiǎn, fèngxì zhōng de juélèi zhíwù, shènzhì yǒu jǐ zhī zhǐ zài yīnyǐng zhōng huódòng de māo. Wǒ bǎ zhèxiē chángnián bú jiàn yángguāng de qūyù chēng wéi 「yǐngzi qúndǎo」, tāmen xiàng sànluò zài chéngshì guāngmíng hǎiyáng zhōng de ànsè xiǎo dǎo. Yí gè qínglǎng de dōngrì wǔhòu, wǒ fāxiàn liǎng dòng lóu de yǐngzi zài jiēdào zhōngyāng duǎnzàn de jiāochā chóngdié, xíngchéng le yí gè gèng shēn de ànyǐng — nà shì liǎng dòng lóu wéiyī de yí cì 「wòshǒu」, měi tiān zhǐ chíxù dàyuē shíwǔ fēnzhōng.
text_en: For a while I became fascinated with observing building shadows. Every day at three in the afternoon, I would stand at the same intersection and record the shadow outlines that surrounding buildings cast on the ground. After a month I had accumulated thirty shadow maps; stacked together, you could clearly see the trajectory of shadows migrating with the seasons. Winter shadows are long and narrow, like slender arms extended by buildings trying to touch the distant ground. Summer shadows shrink to a small patch of darkness at the buildings' feet, as if reluctant to stray too far from their own bodies. What surprised me most was that certain shadow zones were never touched by sunlight throughout the entire day — they are eternal shade zones, damp and cool, nurturing unique micro-ecosystems: moss in wall corners, ferns in crevices, even a few cats that only move about in the shadows. I call these perpetually sunless zones 'shadow archipelagos' — dark little islands scattered in the city's ocean of light. On a clear winter afternoon, I discovered two buildings' shadows briefly crossed and overlapped in the middle of the street, forming an even deeper darkness — the only 'handshake' between the two buildings, lasting only about fifteen minutes each day.

questions.q_zh: 如果去掉这段话的最后一句，意义会怎样变化？
questions.q_en: If the last sentence were removed, how would the meaning change?

options.text_en: the overall feeling would be incomplete

questions.explanation: The final line ('On a clear winter afternoon, I discovered two buildings'...') delivers the thematic payoff; without it, the preceding observations lack their intended resolution.

questions.q_zh: 两栋楼影子的「握手」每天持续多长时间？
questions.q_en: How long does the two buildings' shadow 'handshake' last each day?
questions.explanation: The two buildings' shadows briefly overlap on winter afternoons in a 'handshake' that lasts only about fifteen minutes before separating again.

title: The Sound Map of a Rainy Night
text_zh: 下雨的夜晚躺在床上，我开始辨认每一种雨声的来源和质地。雨打在铁皮雨棚上是一种急促的「噼里啪啦」，节奏密集而缺乏旋律，像一群没有指挥的鼓手同时演奏。雨打在玻璃窗上则柔和得多，发出一种沉闷的「嗒嗒」声，每一滴雨都像一个微小的拳头在礼貌地敲门。阳台上的花盆积了水以后，新落下的雨滴会激起细小的水花，发出一种清脆的高音，和铁皮的低沉形成了音域上的互补。最远处传来的是雨水汇入下水道的哗哗声，那是所有雨滴最终汇聚的终点，像是一条隐形的河流在城市的地下奔涌。偶尔有一辆汽车驶过积水的路面，轮胎溅起水花的声音像是一把巨大的刷子在画布上横扫而过。我闭着眼睛，试着在脑海中绘制一幅声音地图：近处的玻璃窗、中距离的铁皮棚、远处的下水道，它们构成了一个由近及远的声学景观。雨夜的城市并不沉默，它只是换了一种语言在说话。
text_pinyin: Xià yǔ de yèwǎn tǎng zài chuáng shàng, wǒ kāishǐ biànrèn měi yì zhǒng yǔ shēng de láiyuán hé zhìdì. Yǔ dǎ zài tiěpí yǔpéng shàng shì yì zhǒng jícù de 「pīlipālā」, jiézòu mìjí ér quēfá xuánlǜ, xiàng yì qún méiyǒu zhǐhuī de gǔshǒu tóngshí yǎnzòu. Yǔ dǎ zài bōli chuāng shàng zé róuhé de duō, fāchū yì zhǒng chénmèn de 「dādā」 shēng, měi yì dī yǔ dōu xiàng yí gè wēixiǎo de quántou zài lǐmào de qiāo mén. Yángtái shàng de huāpén jī le shuǐ yǐhòu, xīn luò xià de yǔdī huì jī qǐ xìxiǎo de shuǐhuā, fāchū yì zhǒng qīngcuì de gāoyīn, hé tiěpí de dīchén xíngchéng le yīnyù shàng de hùbǔ. Zuì yuǎnchù chuán lái de shì yǔshuǐ huì rù xiàshuǐdào de huāhuā shēng, nà shì suǒyǒu yǔdī zuìzhōng huìjù de zhōngdiǎn, xiàng shì yì tiáo yǐnxíng de héliú zài chéngshì de dìxià bēnyǒng. Ǒu'ěr yǒu yí liàng qìchē shǐ guò jīshuǐ de lùmiàn, lúntāi jiàn qǐ shuǐhuā de shēngyīn xiàng shì yì bǎ jùdà de shuāzi zài huàbù shàng héng sǎo ér guò. Wǒ bì zhe yǎnjīng, shì zhe zài nǎohǎi zhōng huìzhì yì fú shēngyīn dìtú: jìnchù de bōli chuāng, zhōng jùlí de tiěpí péng, yuǎnchù de xiàshuǐdào, tāmen gòuchéng le yí gè yóu jìn jí yuǎn de shēngxué jǐngguān. Yǔ yè de chéngshì bìng bù chénmò, tā zhǐ shì huàn le yì zhǒng yǔyán zài shuōhuà.
text_en: Lying in bed on a rainy night, I begin distinguishing the source and texture of every type of rain sound. Rain on the tin awning is a rapid pitter-patter, rhythmically dense but lacking melody, like a group of drummers playing simultaneously without a conductor. Rain on the glass window is much softer, making a muffled tapping, each raindrop like a tiny fist knocking politely at a door. After water accumulates in the balcony flowerpots, newly falling drops splash tiny sprays of water, producing a crisp high pitch that complements the deep tone of the tin in register. From the farthest distance comes the rushing sound of rainwater flowing into storm drains — the final destination where all raindrops converge, like an invisible river surging beneath the city. Occasionally a car drives over a waterlogged road, and the sound of tires splashing water is like a giant brush sweeping across a canvas. With eyes closed, I try to draw a sound map in my mind: the nearby glass windows, the mid-distance tin awning, the distant storm drains — they compose an acoustic landscape stretching from near to far. A rainy city is not silent; it has merely switched to a different language.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的'留白'（没说的部分）有什么作用？
questions.q_en: What role does the 'white space' (what's unsaid) play in this passage?

options.text_en: gives the reader room to imagine

options.text: 说明作者写作能力不足
options.text_en: shows the author's lack of skill

questions.explanation: The passage catalogs rain sounds without stating the emotional meaning directly; the reader supplies the feelings that the acoustic descriptions evoke.

questions.q_zh: 文中的象征意义是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the symbolic meaning in this text?

options.text: 外在事物反映内心世界
options.text_en: external things reflect the inner world

questions.explanation: Physical details — sounds, textures, weather, objects — serve as proxies for inner states; the author reads external phenomena as expressions of psychological or existential truth.

title: The Patience of a Street Lamp
text_zh: 我家楼下有一盏老式路灯，每天傍晚六点二十三分准时亮起。我之所以知道这么精确的时间，是因为我连续记录了两个月，发现它的启动时间完全由光敏传感器控制，与日落时间精确同步。这盏路灯照亮的范围是一个直径大约八米的圆形光圈，光的边缘并不清晰，而是渐渐过渡到黑暗中，像是水彩画中颜色的晕染。每天晚上，这个光圈就像一个小型剧场的舞台灯光，照亮了一系列微小的夜间演出：九点左右，一只橘色的猫会穿过光圈，它总是沿着光与影的边界行走，似乎在审慎地权衡暴露与隐蔽的利弊。十点半，遛狗的王叔叔会在灯下停留几分钟，等他的老年金毛犬慢慢嗅完电线杆周围的气味信息。深夜一点，偶尔有晚归的年轻人在灯下掏出手机查看地图，他们的脸被屏幕的蓝光和路灯的暖光同时照亮，呈现出一种奇异的双色调。路灯从不催促任何人，也从不评判谁在什么时间经过。它只是每天准时亮起，耐心地守候着这个小小的光明领地。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā lóu xià yǒu yì zhǎn lǎoshì lùdēng, měi tiān bàngwǎn liù diǎn èrshísān fēn zhǔnshí liàng qǐ. Wǒ zhī suǒyǐ zhīdào zhème jīngquè de shíjiān, shì yīnwèi wǒ liánxù jìlù le liǎng gè yuè, fāxiàn tā de qǐdòng shíjiān wánquán yóu guāngmǐn chuángǎnqì kòngzhì, yǔ rìluò shíjiān jīngquè tóngbù. Zhè zhǎn lùdēng zhàoliàng de fànwéi shì yí gè zhíjìng dàyuē bā mǐ de yuánxíng guāngquān, guāng de biānyuán bìng bù qīngxī, ér shì jiànjiàn guòdù dào hēi'àn zhōng, xiàng shì shuǐcǎihuà zhōng yánsè de yūnrǎn. Měi tiān wǎnshàng, zhège guāngquān jiù xiàng yí gè xiǎoxíng jùchǎng de wǔtái dēngguāng, zhàoliàng le yí xìliè wēixiǎo de yèjiān yǎnchū: jiǔ diǎn zuǒyòu, yì zhī júsè de māo huì chuān guò guāngquān, tā zǒng shì yán zhe guāng yǔ yǐng de biānjiè xíngzǒu, sìhū zài shěnshèn de quánhéng bàolù yǔ yǐnbì de lìbì. Shí diǎn bàn, liú gǒu de Wáng shūshu huì zài dēng xià tíngliú jǐ fēnzhōng, děng tā de lǎonián jīnmáo quǎn mànmàn xiù wán diànxiàngān zhōuwéi de qìwèi xìnxī. Shēnyè yì diǎn, ǒu'ěr yǒu wǎn guī de niánqīng rén zài dēng xià tāo chū shǒujī chákàn dìtú, tāmen de liǎn bèi píngmù de lán guāng hé lùdēng de nuǎn guāng tóngshí zhàoliàng, chéngxiàn chū yì zhǒng qíyì de shuāng sèdiào. Lùdēng cóng bù cuīcù rènhé rén, yě cóng bú píngjuàn shéi zài shénme shíjiān jīngguò. Tā zhǐ shì měi tiān zhǔnshí liàng qǐ, nàixīn de shǒuhòu zhe zhège xiǎoxiǎo de guāngmíng lǐngdì.
text_en: There's an old street lamp downstairs that turns on at 6:23 every evening. I know this because I recorded it for two months — its activation is controlled by a photosensitive sensor, synchronized with sunset. It illuminates a circle roughly eight meters across, edges gradually transitioning into darkness like watercolor blending. Every evening this light is like a small theater's stage: around nine, an orange cat crosses through, always walking the boundary of light and shadow. At ten-thirty, Uncle Wang pauses with his dog, waiting for his elderly golden retriever to sniff around the utility pole. At one in the morning, occasionally a young person checks a map under the lamp, face lit simultaneously by the screen's blue and the lamp's warm light. The street lamp never rushes anyone, nor judges who passes at what hour. It turns on every day, keeping watch over its small territory of light.

questions.q_zh: 路灯的启动时间由什么控制？
questions.q_en: What controls the street lamp's activation time?
questions.explanation: A photosensitive sensor controls the lamp, which is why its activation time shifts by a few minutes each day as daylight hours change seasonally.

questions.q_zh: 橘色的猫在路灯下有什么特别的行为？
questions.q_en: What special behavior does the orange cat display under the lamp?

options.text_en: Sleeps in the center of the light

options.text: 沿着光与影的边界行走
options.pinyin: yán zhe guāng yǔ yǐng de biānjiè xíngzǒu
options.text_en: Walks along the boundary of light and shadow

options.pinyin: hé Wáng shūshu de gǒu yìqǐ wán

questions.explanation: An orange cat treats the lamp's light-shadow boundary as a tightrope, walking precisely along the edge each evening like a performer on stage.

title: The Conversation Between Walls
text_zh: 老城区有一条特别窄的巷子，两面墙壁之间的距离不到一米。我每次走过这条巷子，都会注意到两面墙上的痕迹似乎在进行一场跨越时间的对话。左边的墙是一栋民国时期的砖房，红砖已经风化成了深褐色，砖缝之间的灰浆有些地方已经脱落，露出了里面黑色的空洞。右边的墙则属于一栋八十年代的混凝土建筑，表面涂着逐渐剥落的白色石灰，下面隐约可见灰色的水泥。两面墙上都有水渍的痕迹，但流向截然不同：左边墙上的水渍从上往下蜿蜒，像一棵倒挂的树；右边墙上的水渍则是横向扩展的，像一片平铺的云层。我推测这是因为两面墙的材质和表面粗糙度不同，导致雨水在上面的流动方式也不一样。在巷子的最窄处，两面墙上各有一片颜色发绿的区域——那是雨天积水时飞溅到墙面上的水痕催生出的藻类，它们隔着不到六十厘米的距离遥遥相对，像两个素未谋面却因为相同环境而长出了相同容貌的陌生人。
text_pinyin: Lǎo chéngqū yǒu yì tiáo tèbié zhǎi de xiàngzi, liǎng miàn qiángbì zhī jiān de jùlí bú dào yì mǐ. Wǒ měi cì zǒu guò zhè tiáo xiàngzi, dōu huì zhùyì dào liǎng miàn qiáng shàng de hénjì sìhū zài jìnxíng yì chǎng kuàyuè shíjiān de duìhuà. Zuǒbiān de qiáng shì yí dòng Mínguó shíqī de zhuān fáng, hóng zhuān yǐjīng fēnghuà chéng le shēn hésè, zhuān fèng zhī jiān de huījiāng yǒuxiē dìfāng yǐjīng tuōluò, lùchū le lǐmiàn hēisè de kōngdòng. Yòubiān de qiáng zé shǔyú yí dòng bāshí niándài de hùnníngtǔ jiànzhù, biǎomiàn tú zhe zhújiàn bōluò de báisè shíhuī, xiàmiàn yǐnyuē kějiàn huīsè de shuǐní. Liǎng miàn qiáng shàng dōu yǒu shuǐzì de hénjì, dàn liúxiàng jiérán bùtóng: zuǒbiān qiáng shàng de shuǐzì cóng shàng wǎng xià wānyán, xiàng yì kē dào guà de shù; yòubiān qiáng shàng de shuǐzì zé shì héngxiàng kuòzhǎn de, xiàng yí piàn píng pū de yúncéng. Wǒ tuīcè zhè shì yīnwèi liǎng miàn qiáng de cáizhì hé biǎomiàn cūcāodù bùtóng, dǎozhì yǔshuǐ zài shàngmiàn de liúdòng fāngshì yě bù yíyàng. Zài xiàngzi de zuì zhǎi chù, liǎng miàn qiáng shàng gè yǒu yí piàn yánsè fā lǜ de qūyù — nà shì yǔ tiān jīshuǐ shí fēijiàn dào qiáng miàn shàng de shuǐhén cuīshēng chū de zǎolèi, tāmen gé zhe bú dào liùshí límǐ de jùlí yáoyáo xiāngduì, xiàng liǎng gè sù wèi móumiàn què yīnwèi xiāngtóng huánjìng ér zhǎng chū le xiāngtóng róngmào de mòshēngrén.
text_en: In the old city there's a particularly narrow alley, with less than a meter between the two walls. Every time I walk through this alley, I notice the marks on the two walls seem to be conducting a conversation across time. The left wall belongs to a Republican-era brick building, its red bricks weathered to deep brown, the mortar between bricks having fallen away in places to reveal dark hollows inside. The right wall belongs to an 1980s concrete building, its surface coated with gradually peeling white lime, through which gray cement is faintly visible. Both walls bear water stain marks, but flowing in entirely different directions: the stains on the left wall meander downward, like an upside-down tree; the stains on the right wall expand horizontally, like a flat layer of clouds. I speculate this is because the two walls differ in material and surface roughness, causing rainwater to flow differently on each. At the narrowest point of the alley, each wall has a greenish patch — algae spawned by water splashing onto the walls during rainy pooling, facing each other across less than sixty centimeters, like two strangers who have never met yet grew identical features because of the same environment.

questions.q_zh: 左边墙上的水渍呈什么形状？
questions.q_en: What shape do the water stains on the left wall resemble?

options.pinyin: yì kē dào guà de shù

questions.explanation: The water stains on the left wall flow in branching patterns that resemble an upside-down tree, contrasting with the right wall's different stain shapes.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的写作手法有什么特点？
questions.q_en: What is distinctive about the writing technique in this passage?

options.text: 以小见大，用细节传达深意
options.pinyin: yǐ xiǎo jiàn dà, yòng xìjié chuándá shēnyì
options.text_en: uses small details to convey big meaning

questions.explanation: The passage extracts large thematic meaning from minute physical observations — wear marks, textures, small behaviors — using the particular to illuminate the universal.

title: The Archaeology of a Drawer
text_zh: 整理书桌抽屉的时候，我突然意识到这个抽屉就像一个微型的考古遗址——越往下层翻，东西越古老。最上面一层是最近几天的物品：一支用了一半的笔、几张便签纸、一个充电线缠绕在一起的耳机。第二层开始出现一些遗忘已久的东西：一张三个月前的电影票根、一把不知道开什么锁的钥匙、一包已经过期的润喉糖。第三层几乎可以称为「文化层」了：一本两年前的小笔记本，里面记着当时学习外语的单词，字迹工整而陌生，仿佛出自另一个人之手。最底层是真正的「远古文物」——一张褪色的照片、一枚纪念币、一块已经干透的橡皮泥。每一件物品都标记着我生活中某个已经结束的阶段，它们沉入抽屉底部的方式和文物被泥土覆盖的过程惊人地相似：不是被刻意埋葬的，而是被新的日常生活一层一层地覆盖，直到被彻底遗忘。我小心翼翼地把每一件东西拿出来端详，然后又按照原来的顺序放了回去——就像一个考古学家在记录完遗址以后选择了原地保存。
text_pinyin: Zhěnglǐ shūzhuō chōuti de shíhou, wǒ tūrán yìshí dào zhège chōuti jiù xiàng yí gè wēixíng de kǎogǔ yízhǐ — yuè wǎng xià céng fān, dōngxi yuè gǔlǎo. Zuì shàngmiàn yì céng shì zuìjìn jǐ tiān de wùpǐn: yì zhī yòng le yíbàn de bǐ, jǐ zhāng biànqiān zhǐ, yí gè chōngdiàn xiàn chánrào zài yìqǐ de ěrjī. Dì èr céng kāishǐ chūxiàn yìxiē yíwàng yǐ jiǔ de dōngxi: yì zhāng sān gè yuè qián de diànyǐng piào gēn, yì bǎ bù zhīdào kāi shénme suǒ de yàoshi, yì bāo yǐjīng guòqī de rùnhóu táng. Dì sān céng jīhū kěyǐ chēng wéi 「wénhuà céng」 le: yì běn liǎng nián qián de xiǎo bǐjìběn, lǐmiàn jì zhe dāngshí xuéxí wàiyǔ de dāncí, zìjì gōngzhěng ér mòshēng, fǎngfú chūzì lìng yí gè rén zhī shǒu. Zuì dǐcéng shì zhēnzhèng de 「yuǎngǔ wénwù」 — yì zhāng tuìsè de zhàopiàn, yì méi jìniànbì, yí kuài yǐjīng gān tòu de xiàngpíní. Měi yí jiàn wùpǐn dōu biāojì zhe wǒ shēnghuó zhōng mǒu gè yǐjīng jiéshù de jiēduàn, tāmen chén rù chōuti dǐbù de fāngshì hé wénwù bèi nítǔ fùgài de guòchéng jīngrén de xiāngsì: bú shì bèi kèyì máizàng de, ér shì bèi xīn de rìcháng shēnghuó yì céng yì céng de fùgài, zhídào bèi chèdǐ yíwàng. Wǒ xiǎoxīnyìyì de bǎ měi yí jiàn dōngxi ná chūlái duānxiáng, ránhòu yòu ànzhào yuánlái de shùnxù fàng le huíqù — jiù xiàng yí gè kǎogǔ xuéjiā zài jìlù wán yízhǐ yǐhòu xuǎnzé le yuándì bǎocún.
text_en: While organizing my desk drawer, this drawer is like a miniature archaeological site — the deeper you dig, the older things get. The top layer contains items from the last few days: a half-used pen, some sticky notes, earphones with charging cable tangled together. The second layer reveals long-forgotten things: a movie ticket stub from three months ago, a key that opens who-knows-what lock, a pack of already-expired throat lozenges. The third layer can almost be called a 'cultural stratum': a small notebook from two years ago with foreign language vocabulary written inside, the handwriting neat yet unfamiliar, as if from another person's hand. The bottom layer holds true 'ancient artifacts' — a faded photograph, a commemorative coin, a piece of completely dried-out modeling clay. Each item marks some already-concluded phase of my life, and the way they sank to the bottom of the drawer is strikingly similar to how artifacts are covered by soil: not deliberately buried, but covered layer by layer by new daily life until completely forgotten. I carefully take each item out to examine it, then put everything back in its original order — like an archaeologist who, after documenting a site, chooses to preserve it in place.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以看出什么样的关系？
questions.q_en: What kind of relationship does this passage reveal?
questions.explanation: The layered drawer contents map a personal history; the author's care in examining and returning each item reveals a genuine, intimate relationship with their own past.

questions.q_zh: 作者最后怎么处理了抽屉里的东西？
questions.q_en: What did the author ultimately do with the drawer items?

options.text: 按照原来的顺序放了回去
options.pinyin: ànzhào yuánlái de shùnxù fàng le huíqù
options.text_en: Put them back in original order

options.pinyin: fàng dào le xīn de hézi lǐ
options.text_en: Put them in a new box

questions.explanation: After examining each item and the memories it held, the author carefully returned everything to its original position — choosing preservation over change.

title: The Weather Inside a Teacup
text_zh: 泡茶的时候我喜欢观察杯子里的「天气变化」。刚倒入开水的瞬间，茶叶在杯中剧烈翻腾，像是一场微型风暴。几秒钟以后，翻腾平息，水面升起一层薄薄的蒸汽——那是茶杯世界里的云层。如果房间里有微弱的气流，这层蒸汽会朝一个方向缓缓漂移，就像真实天空中被风推动的云朵。茶叶在沉降过程中也有自己的气象学：较轻的叶片在中层漂浮旋转，像是被热对流托起的落叶；较重的叶梗则迅速沉入杯底，安静地躺在那里，形成一片微型的「沉积层」。最迷人的是茶汤颜色的变化——从最初的透明，经过淡黄、琥珀，到最后的深褐，这个过程就像一天之中天空颜色从黎明到黄昏的渐变。如果你耐心等待足够长的时间，茶汤表面会形成一层极薄的氧化膜，在灯光下呈现出虹彩般的光泽，那是茶杯宇宙里的极光。每一杯茶都是一个完整的气象循环，从风暴开始，以宁静结束。
text_pinyin: Pào chá de shíhou wǒ xǐhuan guānchá bēizi lǐ de 「tiānqì biànhuà」. Gāng dào rù kāishuǐ de shùnjiān, cháyè zài bēi zhōng jùliè fānténg, xiàng shì yì chǎng wēixíng fēngbào. Jǐ miǎozhōng yǐhòu, fānténg píngxī, shuǐmiàn shēng qǐ yì céng báobáo de zhēngqì — nà shì chábēi shìjiè lǐ de yúncéng. Rúguǒ fángjiān lǐ yǒu wēiruò de qìliú, zhè céng zhēngqì huì cháo yí gè fāngxiàng huǎnhuǎn piāoyí, jiù xiàng zhēnshí tiānkōng zhōng bèi fēng tuīdòng de yúnduǒ. Cháyè zài chénjiàng guòchéng zhōng yě yǒu zìjǐ de qìxiàngxué: jiào qīng de yèpiàn zài zhōng céng piāofú xuánzhuǎn, xiàng shì bèi rè duìliú tuō qǐ de luòyè; jiào zhòng de yègěng zé xùnsù chén rù bēi dǐ, ānjìng de tǎng zài nàlǐ, xíngchéng yí piàn wēixíng de 「chénjī céng」. Zuì mírén de shì chá tāng yánsè de biànhuà — cóng zuìchū de tòumíng, jīngguò dàn huáng, hǔpò, dào zuìhòu de shēn hè, zhège guòchéng jiù xiàng yì tiān zhī zhōng tiānkōng yánsè cóng límíng dào huánghūn de jiànbiàn. Rúguǒ nǐ nàixīn děngdài zúgòu cháng de shíjiān, chá tāng biǎomiàn huì xíngchéng yì céng jí báo de yǎnghuà mó, zài dēngguāng xià chéngxiàn chū hóngcǎi bān de guāngzé, nà shì chábēi yǔzhòu lǐ de jíguāng. Měi yì bēi chá dōu shì yí gè wánzhěng de qìxiàng xúnhuán, cóng fēngbào kāishǐ, yǐ níngjìng jiéshù.
text_en: When making tea I like to observe the 'weather changes' inside the cup. The instant boiling water is poured in, the tea leaves churn violently in the cup, like a miniature storm. A few seconds later the turbulence subsides, and a thin layer of steam rises from the surface — the cloud layer of the teacup world. If there's a faint draft in the room, this steam drifts slowly in one direction, just like real clouds pushed by wind across the sky. The tea leaves have their own meteorology during their descent: lighter leaves float and spin at mid-level, like fallen leaves lifted by thermal convection; heavier stems quickly sink to the bottom, lying quietly there, forming a miniature 'sediment layer.' Most enchanting is the change in tea color — from initial transparency, through pale yellow, amber, to final deep brown, a process like the gradual shift of sky color from dawn to dusk over a single day. If you wait patiently long enough, the tea surface forms an extremely thin oxidation film that under lamplight displays an iridescent sheen — the aurora of the teacup universe. Every cup of tea is a complete weather cycle, beginning with a storm and ending in tranquility.

questions.q_zh: 这段话中最关键的转折是什么？
questions.q_en: What is the most important turning point in this passage?
questions.explanation: The passage shifts from pure physical observation to philosophical reflection, marking the moment when sensory data becomes personal meaning — a change in awareness, not setting.

questions.q_zh: 如果去掉这段话的最后一句，意义会怎样变化？
questions.q_en: If the last sentence were removed, how would the meaning change?

options.text_en: the overall feeling would be incomplete

questions.explanation: The final line ('Every cup of tea is a complete weather cycle, beginning...') delivers the thematic payoff; without it, the preceding observations lack their intended resolution.

title: The Biography of a Crack
text_zh: 卧室天花板上有一条裂缝，从东南角蜿蜒向西北方向延伸了大约四十厘米。我搬进来的时候它只有十厘米长，三年来我一直在默默地追踪它的生长轨迹。这条裂缝并不是匀速扩展的——冬天暖气运行的时候，干燥的空气会加速裂缝的生长，平均每周能推进两到三毫米；而潮湿的夏天，裂缝几乎停止了生长，仿佛在雨季休眠。裂缝的走向也很有讲究：它并不走直线，而是沿着天花板涂层下面隐藏的施工缝隙曲折前进，就像一条河流总是沿着地势最低的路线流淌。在一个特别干燥的冬天，裂缝突然分出了一条支流——一个更细的裂纹从主裂缝的中段向南偏转，延伸了大约五厘米后停住了。我用铅笔在裂缝的末端做了标记，每个月测量一次进度。有朋友来做客看到天花板上密密麻麻的铅笔标记，问我那是什么。我说那是一条河流的地图，一条用三年时间流过我天花板的极慢的河流。
text_pinyin: Wòshì tiānhuābǎn shàng yǒu yì tiáo lièfèng, cóng dōngnán jiǎo wānyán xiàng xīběi fāngxiàng yánshēn le dàyuē sìshí límǐ. Wǒ bān jìnlái de shíhou tā zhǐ yǒu shí límǐ cháng, sān nián lái wǒ yìzhí zài mòmò de zhuīzōng tā de shēngzhǎng guǐjì. Zhè tiáo lièfèng bìng bú shì yúnsù kuòzhǎn de — dōngtiān nuǎnqì yùnxíng de shíhou, gānzào de kōngqì huì jiāsù lièfèng de shēngzhǎng, píngjūn měi zhōu néng tuījìn liǎng dào sān háomǐ; ér cháoshī de xiàtiān, lièfèng jīhū tíngzhǐ le shēngzhǎng, fǎngfú zài yǔjì xiūmián. Lièfèng de zǒuxiàng yě hěn yǒu jiǎngjiù: tā bìng bù zǒu zhíxiàn, ér shì yán zhe tiānhuābǎn túcéng xiàmiàn yǐncáng de shīgōng fèngxì qūzhé qiánjìn, jiù xiàng yì tiáo héliú zǒng shì yán zhe dìshì zuìdī de lùxiàn liútǎng. Zài yí gè tèbié gānzào de dōngtiān, lièfèng tūrán fēn chū le yì tiáo zhīliú — yí gè gèng xì de lièwén cóng zhǔ lièfèng de zhōngduàn xiàng nán piānzhuǎn, yánshēn le dàyuē wǔ límǐ hòu tíng zhù le. Wǒ yòng qiānbǐ zài lièfèng de mòduān zuò le biāojì, měi gè yuè cèliáng yí cì jìndù. Yǒu péngyou lái zuòkè kàn dào tiānhuābǎn shàng mìmimámá de qiānbǐ biāojì, wèn wǒ nà shì shénme. Wǒ shuō nà shì yì tiáo héliú de dìtú, yì tiáo yòng sān nián shíjiān liú guò wǒ tiānhuābǎn de jí màn de héliú.
text_en: There's a crack on my bedroom ceiling that meanders from the southeast corner toward the northwest, extending about forty centimeters. When I moved in it was only ten centimeters long; over three years I've been silently tracking its growth trajectory. This crack doesn't expand at a constant rate — when the heating runs in winter, dry air accelerates its growth, advancing an average of two to three millimeters per week; but in humid summer, the crack nearly stops growing, as if hibernating during the rainy season. The crack's direction is also deliberate: it doesn't go straight but zigzags along hidden construction seams beneath the ceiling coating, like a river that always flows along the lowest terrain. During one particularly dry winter, the crack suddenly branched — a finer fracture line diverged south from the main crack's midsection, extending about five centimeters before stopping. I marked the crack's endpoint in pencil and measured progress monthly. When a friend visited and saw the dense pencil marks on the ceiling, they asked what they were. I said it was a map of a river — an extremely slow river that took three years to flow across my ceiling.

questions.q_zh: 什么季节裂缝生长最快？
questions.q_en: In which season does the crack grow fastest?
questions.explanation: The relevant passage section ('but in humid summer, the crack nearly stops growing, as if...') provides the answer directly; alternatives contradict stated facts.

questions.q_zh: 这段话的写作手法有什么特点？
questions.q_en: What is distinctive about the writing technique in this passage?

options.text: 以小见大，用细节传达深意
options.pinyin: yǐ xiǎo jiàn dà, yòng xìjié chuándá shēnyì
options.text_en: uses small details to convey big meaning

questions.explanation: The passage extracts large thematic meaning from minute physical observations — wear marks, textures, small behaviors — using the particular to illuminate the universal.

title: The Orchestra of a Kitchen Timer
text_zh: 我的厨房里同时有三个定时器在运行的时候，就像一个微型交响乐团在演奏。烤箱的定时器发出低沉而稳定的嗡嗡声，像大提琴维持着整首曲子的基调。电饭煲的倒计时则是一种间歇性的电子「哔哔」声，每隔十秒响一次，像是节拍器为整个厨房的运作标注着时间刻度。最后是微波炉的旋转托盘发出的轻微摩擦声，频率不高但持续不断，像是小提琴在背景中拉着一个悠长的持续音。当烤箱定时器率先响起的时候，它的蜂鸣声急促而尖锐，打断了厨房的常规旋律——这是乐章的高潮部分。我关掉烤箱，戴上隔热手套取出烤盘，整个动作行云流水。电饭煲在三分钟后跟进，发出一连串温和的提示音，像是第二乐章的柔板段落。微波炉最后才「叮」的一声宣告完成，简洁而决绝，像是终曲的最后一个音符。三道菜同时完成的时刻，厨房陷入了一种突然的安静，只剩下抽油烟机持续运转的白噪音——那是散场以后还没关掉的空调声。
text_pinyin: Wǒ de chúfáng lǐ tóngshí yǒu sān gè dìngshíqì zài yùnxíng de shíhou, jiù xiàng yí gè wēixíng jiāoxiǎng yuètuán zài yǎnzòu. Kǎoxiāng de dìngshíqì fāchū dīchén ér wěndìng de wēngwēng shēng, xiàng dàtíqín wéichí zhe zhěng shǒu qǔzi de jīdiào. Diànfànbāo de dào jìshí zé shì yì zhǒng jiānxiēxìng de diànzǐ 「bībī」 shēng, měi gé shí miǎo xiǎng yí cì, xiàng shì jiépāiqì wèi zhěng gè chúfáng de yùnzuò biāozhù zhe shíjiān kèdù. Zuìhòu shì wēibōlú de xuánzhuǎn tuōpán fāchū de qīngwēi mócā shēng, pínlǜ bù gāo dàn chíxù bú duàn, xiàng shì xiǎotíqín zài bèijǐng zhōng lā zhe yí gè yōucháng de chíxù yīn. Dāng kǎoxiāng dìngshíqì shuàixiān xiǎng qǐ de shíhou, tā de fēngmíng shēng jícù ér jiānruì, dǎduàn le chúfáng de chángguī xuánlǜ — zhè shì yuèzhāng de gāocháo bùfen. Wǒ guān diào kǎoxiāng, dài shàng gérè shǒutào qǔ chū kǎopán, zhěng gè dòngzuò xíngyún liúshuǐ. Diànfànbāo zài sān fēnzhōng hòu gēnjìn, fāchū yì liánchuàn wēnhé de tíshì yīn, xiàng shì dì èr yuèzhāng de róubǎn duànluò. Wēibōlú zuìhòu cái 「dīng」 de yì shēng xuāngào wánchéng, jiǎnjié ér juéjué, xiàng shì zhōngqǔ de zuìhòu yí gè yīnfú. Sān dào cài tóngshí wánchéng de shíkè, chúfáng xiàn rù le yì zhǒng tūrán de ānjìng, zhǐ shèng xià chōu yóuyānjī chíxù yùnzhuǎn de bái zàoyīn — nà shì sànchǎng yǐhòu hái méi guān diào de kōngtiáo shēng.
text_en: When three timers are running simultaneously in my kitchen, it's like a miniature orchestra performing. The oven timer emits a low, steady hum, like a cello maintaining the keynote of the entire piece. The rice cooker's countdown is an intermittent electronic beeping, sounding once every ten seconds, like a metronome marking time intervals for the entire kitchen's operation. Finally there's the slight friction sound from the microwave's rotating tray, infrequent but continuous, like a violin sustaining a long note in the background. When the oven timer goes off first, its buzzing is urgent and sharp, interrupting the kitchen's regular melody — this is the climax of the movement. I turn off the oven, put on heat-resistant gloves and take out the baking tray, the whole motion fluid and seamless. The rice cooker follows three minutes later with a gentle series of notification tones, like the adagio passage of the second movement. The microwave is last to announce completion with a single 'ding,' concise and decisive, like the final note of the finale. The moment all three dishes finish simultaneously, the kitchen falls into sudden quiet, with only the range hood's continuous white noise remaining — like the air conditioning left running after the audience has gone.

questions.q_zh: 烤箱定时器的声音被比作什么乐器？
questions.q_en: What instrument is the oven timer compared to?
questions.explanation: The relevant passage section ('The oven timer emits a low, steady hum, like a cello maintaining the...') provides the answer directly; alternatives contradict stated facts.

questions.q_zh: 作者选择这个视角有什么效果？
questions.q_en: What effect does the author's choice of perspective have?
questions.explanation: The first-person observational perspective creates sensory intimacy, drawing the reader into direct experience rather than maintaining analytical distance.

title: The Geometry of Fallen Leaves
text_zh: 秋天的人行道上，落叶的分布并不是随机的。我花了一个星期的时间在同一条街上观察落叶的堆积规律，发现了几种反复出现的模式。首先，落叶会在建筑物的背风面聚集，形成弧形的堆积带——风无法把叶子吹过建筑物的拐角，于是它们就堆积在气流减速的区域。其次，路面上的任何微小障碍物都会成为落叶的「收集器」：一个下水道井盖的边缘、一根电线杆的底座、甚至一个被丢弃的塑料瓶，都会在其下风方向积累一小堆落叶。我把这些堆积点标注在地图上，发现它们形成了一个不规则的网络，揭示了这条街上不可见的风场结构。最让我惊讶的发现是：同一棵银杏树落下的叶子，会根据叶子的大小和含水量落在不同的距离上——较大的干燥叶子被风吹得更远，而较小的湿叶子几乎直接落在树的正下方。一棵树的落叶范围就像一个扇形的散射图，每一片叶子的最终位置都是重力、风力和叶片阻力三者博弈的结果。
text_pinyin: Qiūtiān de rénxíngdào shàng, luòyè de fēnbù bìng bú shì suíjī de. Wǒ huā le yí gè xīngqī de shíjiān zài tóng yì tiáo jiē shàng guānchá luòyè de duījī guīlǜ, fāxiàn le jǐ zhǒng fǎnfù chūxiàn de móshì. Shǒuxiān, luòyè huì zài jiànzhùwù de bèi fēng miàn jùjí, xíngchéng húxíng de duījī dài — fēng wúfǎ bǎ yèzi chuī guò jiànzhùwù de guǎijiǎo, yúshì tāmen jiù duījī zài qìliú jiǎnsù de qūyù. Qícì, lùmiàn shàng de rènhé wēixiǎo zhàng'àiwù dōu huì chéngwéi luòyè de 「shōujíqì」: yí gè xiàshuǐdào jǐnggài de biānyuán, yì gēn diànxiàngān de dǐzuò, shènzhì yí gè bèi diūqì de sùliào píng, dōu huì zài qí xiàfēng fāngxiàng jīlěi yì xiǎo duī luòyè. Wǒ bǎ zhèxiē duījī diǎn biāozhù zài dìtú shàng, fāxiàn tāmen xíngchéng le yí gè bù guīzé de wǎngluò, jiēshì le zhè tiáo jiē shàng bùkějiàn de fēngchǎng jiégòu. Zuì ràng wǒ jīngyà de fāxiàn shì: tóng yì kē yínxìng shù luò xià de yèzi, huì gēnjù yèzi de dàxiǎo hé hánshuǐliàng luò zài bùtóng de jùlí shàng — jiào dà de gānzào yèzi bèi fēng chuī de gèng yuǎn, ér jiào xiǎo de shī yèzi jīhū zhíjiē luò zài shù de zhèng xiàfāng. Yì kē shù de luòyè fànwéi jiù xiàng yí gè shànxíng de sǎnshè tú, měi yí piàn yèzi de zuìzhōng wèizhì dōu shì zhònglì, fēnglì hé yèpiàn zǔlì sānzhě bóyì de jiéguǒ.
text_en: On autumn sidewalks, the distribution of fallen leaves is not random. I spent a week observing leaf accumulation patterns on the same street and found several recurring patterns. First, leaves gather on the leeward side of buildings, forming arc-shaped accumulation bands — wind can't blow leaves around building corners, so they pile up where airflow decelerates. Second, any tiny obstacle on the road surface becomes a leaf 'collector': the edge of a manhole cover, the base of a utility pole, even a discarded plastic bottle — all accumulate a small pile of leaves on their downwind side. I marked these accumulation points on a map and found they formed an irregular network, revealing the invisible wind field structure of this street. My most surprising discovery was that leaves from the same ginkgo tree land at different distances based on size and moisture content — larger dry leaves are blown farther by wind, while smaller wet leaves fall almost directly below the tree. A tree's leaf-fall range is like a fan-shaped scatter plot, each leaf's final position the result of a three-way contest between gravity, wind force, and leaf drag.

questions.q_en: Why do fallen leaves accumulate on the leeward side of buildings?

options.text: 风无法把叶子吹过拐角，气流在那里减速
options.pinyin: fēng wúfǎ bǎ yèzi chuī guò guǎijiǎo, qìliú zài nàlǐ jiǎnsù
options.text_en: Wind can't blow leaves past corners, airflow decelerates there

options.pinyin: yǒu rén zhuānmén dǎsǎo dào nàlǐ
shùmù dōu zhòng zài nà yì cè
options.text_en: Trees are all planted on that side

questions.explanation: The passage explains: 'First, leaves gather on the leeward side of buildings, forming...' — this reasoning directly supports the answer; alternatives are unsupported.

questions.q_zh: 同一棵银杏树的叶子，哪种叶子落得离树最远？
questions.q_en: From the same ginkgo tree, which leaves land farthest from the tree?

options.text: 所有叶子落在同一距离
options.pinyin: suǒyǒu yèzi luò zài tóng yí jùlí
options.text_en: All leaves land at the same distance

questions.explanation: The passage evidence ('My most surprising discovery was that leaves from the same...') supports this answer; alternatives either contradict stated details or lack textual basis.

title: The Secret Life of an Elevator
text_zh: 办公楼的电梯在不同时段有完全不同的性格。早晨八点到九点之间，电梯几乎只执行「上行」任务——它在一楼停留的时间最长，等待涌入的上班族把轿厢塞满，然后一路上行，在每个楼层吐出几个人。这时候的电梯是一个效率至上的运输工具，人们面无表情地盯着楼层显示屏，整个轿厢弥漫着咖啡和早餐三明治的气味。中午十二点左右，电梯进入一种混乱的「双向通行」模式：有人下楼去吃饭，有人吃完饭回来，上下交替频繁，电梯几乎在每层楼都要停靠。这时候轿厢里的对话变得轻松起来，偶尔有人讨论附近哪家餐厅今天的午餐特价。下午五点半以后，电梯进入了我最喜欢的阶段——一种缓慢的、带着倦意的下行节奏。人们的脚步不再急促，有人靠在轿厢壁上闭眼休息几秒钟，有人低头发短信。深夜十一点，如果你还在加班，独自乘坐空荡荡的电梯下楼，你会发现电梯终于有了属于自己的声音：钢缆的轻微颤动、轿厢与井道之间的气流声、层门关闭时的机械咔嗒——这些声音白天被人声掩盖了，只有在夜晚才能听见。
text_pinyin: Bàngōnglóu de diàntī zài bùtóng shíduàn yǒu wánquán bùtóng de xìnggé. Zǎochén bā diǎn dào jiǔ diǎn zhī jiān, diàntī jīhū zhǐ zhíxíng 「shàngxíng」 rènwu — tā zài yī lóu tíngliú de shíjiān zuìcháng, děngdài yǒng rù de shàngbānzú bǎ jiàoxiāng sāi mǎn, ránhòu yílù shàngxíng, zài měi gè lóucéng tǔ chū jǐ gè rén. Zhè shíhou de diàntī shì yí gè xiàolǜ zhìshàng de yùnshū gōngjù, rénmen miàn wú biǎoqíng de dīng zhe lóucéng xiǎnshì píng, zhěng gè jiàoxiāng mímàn zhe kāfēi hé zǎocān sānmíngzhì de qìwèi. Zhōngwǔ shí'èr diǎn zuǒyòu, diàntī jìnrù yì zhǒng hǔnluàn de 「shuāngxiàng tōngxíng」 móshì: yǒu rén xià lóu qù chīfàn, yǒu rén chī wán fàn huílái, shàng xià jiāotì pínfán, diàntī jīhū zài měi céng lóu dōu yào tíngkào. Zhè shíhou jiàoxiāng lǐ de duìhuà biàn de qīngsōng qǐlái, ǒu'ěr yǒu rén tǎolùn fùjìn nǎ jiā cāntīng jīntiān de wǔcān tèjià. Xiàwǔ wǔ diǎn bàn yǐhòu, diàntī jìnrù le wǒ zuì xǐhuan de jiēduàn — yì zhǒng huǎnmàn de, dài zhe juànyì de xiàxíng jiézòu. Rénmen de jiǎobù bú zài jícù, yǒu rén kào zài jiàoxiāng bì shàng bì yǎn xiūxi jǐ miǎozhōng, yǒu rén dītóu fā duǎnxìn. Shēnyè shíyī diǎn, rúguǒ nǐ hái zài jiābān, dúzì chéngzuò kōngdàngdàng de diàntī xià lóu, nǐ huì fāxiàn diàntī zhōngyú yǒu le shǔyú zìjǐ de shēngyīn: gānglǎn de qīngwēi chàndòng, jiàoxiāng yǔ jǐngdào zhī jiān de qìliú shēng, céng mén guānbì shí de jīxiè kādā — zhèxiē shēngyīn báitiān bèi rénshēng yǎngài le, zhǐ yǒu zài yèwǎn cái néng tīngjiàn.
text_en: The office elevator has different personalities at different hours. Between eight and nine, it performs only 'ascending' tasks — lingering on the first floor, filling with workers, releasing a few at each stop. People stare at the floor display, the cab permeated with coffee and breakfast sandwich smell. Around noon, it enters chaotic bidirectional mode: some go down for lunch, others return, stopping at almost every floor. Conversations turn relaxed; someone discusses a nearby restaurant's special. After five-thirty, the elevator enters a slow, weary downward rhythm. Footsteps are no longer rushed; someone leans against the wall and closes their eyes, someone bows their head texting. At eleven at night, riding the empty elevator down alone, you discover its own voice: the trembling of cables, airflow between cab and shaft, the mechanical click of doors closing — sounds masked by human voices during the day, audible only now.

questions.q_zh: 早上的电梯主要执行什么任务？
questions.q_en: What task does the elevator mainly perform in the morning?

options.pinyin: tíng zài yī lóu bú dòng

questions.explanation: During the 8-to-9 a.m. rush, the elevator exclusively performs ascending trips as office workers arrive, with no one yet heading down.

questions.q_zh: 深夜的空电梯里能听到什么声音？
questions.q_en: What sounds can be heard in the empty elevator late at night?

options.text: 钢缆颤动、气流声和机械咔嗒
options.pinyin: gānglǎn chàndòng, qìliú shēng hé jīxiè kādā
options.text_en: Cable trembling, airflow, and mechanical clicks

questions.explanation: The passage evidence ('At eleven at night, riding the empty elevator down alone, you...') supports this answer; alternatives either contradict stated details or lack textual basis.

title: The Tide Marks of a Bookshelf
text_zh: 仔细观察一个人的书架，你能看到时间留下的「潮汐线」。我的书架从左到右大致按照购买时间排列，于是形成了一条清晰的阅读兴趣变迁线索。最左边是大学时代买的书——文学理论和哲学入门，书脊上贴着二手书店的价格标签，纸张已经泛黄。中间一段是工作头几年的书——实用性明显增强，商业管理和行业报告占了大多数，很多书看上去几乎是全新的，说明买来以后并没有真正读完。再往右是三十岁以后的书，种类变得丰富起来：几本园艺指南、一些旅行随笔、两三本科普著作，仿佛主人终于从功利性阅读中解脱出来，开始重新为了纯粹的好奇心而读书。最右边是最近一年的书，我注意到其中诗集的比例明显增加了——这或许是人到中年以后对语言精确性的重新渴望。书架的最高一层放着几本永远不会被整理的书，它们没有按任何逻辑排列：是那些被反复翻阅、随手放回、位置不断变化的最爱之书。它们就像潮汐中的漂流物，不属于任何固定的岸线。
text_pinyin: Zǐxì guānchá yí gè rén de shūjià, nǐ néng kàn dào shíjiān liú xià de 「cháoxī xiàn」. Wǒ de shūjià cóng zuǒ dào yòu dàzhì ànzhào gòumǎi shíjiān páiliè, yúshì xíngchéng le yì tiáo qīngxī de yuèdú xìngqù biànqiān xiànsuǒ. Zuì zuǒbiān shì dàxué shídài mǎi de shū — wénxué lǐlùn hé zhéxué rùmén, shū jǐ shàng tiē zhe èrshǒu shūdiàn de jiàgé biāoqiān, zhǐzhāng yǐjīng fàn huáng. Zhōngjiān yí duàn shì gōngzuò tóu jǐ nián de shū — shíyòngxìng míngxiǎn zēngqiáng, shāngyè guǎnlǐ hé hángyè bàogào zhàn le dà duōshù, hěn duō shū kàn shàngqù jīhū shì quánxīn de, shuōmíng mǎi lái yǐhòu bìng méiyǒu zhēnzhèng dú wán. Zài wǎng yòu shì sānshí suì yǐhòu de shū, zhǒnglèi biàn de fēngfù qǐlái: jǐ běn yuányì zhǐnán, yìxiē lǚxíng suíbǐ, liǎng sān běn kēpǔ zhùzuò, fǎngfú zhǔrén zhōngyú cóng gōnglìxìng yuèdú zhōng jiětuō chūlái, kāishǐ chóngxīn wèile chúncuì de hàoqíxīn ér dúshū. Zuì yòubiān shì zuìjìn yì nián de shū, wǒ zhùyì dào qízhōng shījí de bǐlì míngxiǎn zēngjiā le — zhè huòxǔ shì rén dào zhōngnián yǐhòu duì yǔyán jīngquèxìng de chóngxīn kěwàng. Shūjià de zuìgāo yì céng fàng zhe jǐ běn yǒngyuǎn bú huì bèi zhěnglǐ de shū, tāmen méiyǒu àn rènhé luóji páiliè: shì nàxiē bèi fǎnfù fānyuè, suíshǒu fàng huí, wèizhì búduàn biànhuà de zuì'ài zhī shū. Tāmen jiù xiàng cháoxī zhōng de piāoliúwù, bù shǔyú rènhé gùdìng de ànxiàn.
text_en: Observe someone's bookshelf carefully and you can see the 'tide marks' left by time. My bookshelf is roughly arranged left to right by purchase date, forming a clear trail of shifting reading interests. The far left holds university-era books — literary theory and introductory philosophy, their spines bearing secondhand bookshop price stickers, pages already yellowed. The middle section contains books from the first years of work — noticeably more practical, business management and industry reports making up most of them, many looking virtually brand new, suggesting they were never actually finished after purchase. Further right are books from after age thirty, the variety becoming richer: a few gardening guides, some travel essays, two or three popular science works, as if the owner finally broke free from utilitarian reading and began reading again out of pure curiosity. The far right holds the past year's books, among which I notice the proportion of poetry collections has clearly increased — perhaps a renewed craving for linguistic precision that comes with middle age. The topmost shelf holds a few books that will never be organized, arranged by no logic: those most-loved books that are repeatedly opened, casually replaced, their positions constantly shifting. They are like driftwood in the tides, belonging to no fixed shoreline.

questions.q_zh: 书架中间部分的书有什么特点？
questions.q_en: What characterizes the books in the middle section of the shelf?

options.text: 实用性强，很多看起来像新的
options.pinyin: shíyòngxìng qiáng, hěn duō kàn qǐlái xiàng xīn de

questions.explanation: The passage evidence ('The middle section contains books from the first years of work —...') supports this answer; alternatives either contradict stated details or lack textual basis.

questions.q_zh: 这段话对工作态度的看法是什么？
questions.q_en: What view of work attitude does this passage present?

options.text_en: work is less important than life

questions.explanation: The author's response to their single error — becoming more careful, not less engaged — shows conscientiousness matters as a value in itself, independent of recognition.

title: The Fingerprints of Light
text_zh: 每个房间都有自己独特的光线「指纹」，由窗户的朝向、面积、遮挡物和室内材质共同决定。我的客厅朝南，上午十点左右阳光从窗户射入，在木地板上投射出一个明亮的长方形光斑。这个光斑每天都在缓慢地移动——夏至时它只到达房间的三分之一处，冬至时却能延伸到对面墙壁的脚下。木地板经年累月的阳光照射在不同区域造成了深浅不同的色差：经常被阳光覆盖的区域颜色明显变浅了，而家具遮挡的区域则保持着原来的深色调。如果你搬开一个长期放置的柜子，就能看到地板上清晰的颜色分界线——那是光线日复一日「书写」在木头上的痕迹。我还注意到，午后的阳光穿过窗户上的不同区域会呈现微妙的色温差异：靠近窗框边缘的光线略带暖色，因为经过了窗框表面的反射；而直接穿过玻璃中央的光线则偏向冷白。这些差异肉眼几乎察觉不到，但地板上长年积累的色差忠实地记录了每一条光线的温度和轨迹。
text_pinyin: Měi gè fángjiān dōu yǒu zìjǐ dútè de guāngxiàn 「zhǐwén」, yóu chuānghù de cháoxiàng, miànjī, zhēdǎngwù hé shìnèi cáizhì gòngtóng juédìng. Wǒ de kètīng cháo nán, shàngwǔ shí diǎn zuǒyòu yángguāng cóng chuānghù shè rù, zài mù dìbǎn shàng tóushè chū yí gè míngliàng de chángfāngxíng guāngbān. Zhège guāngbān měi tiān dōu zài huǎnmàn de yídòng — xiàzhì shí tā zhǐ dàodá fángjiān de sān fēn zhī yī chù, dōngzhì shí què néng yánshēn dào duìmiàn qiángbì de jiǎo xià. Mù dìbǎn jīngnián lěiyuè de yángguāng zhàoshè zài bùtóng qūyù zàochéng le shēn qiǎn bùtóng de sèchā: jīngcháng bèi yángguāng fùgài de qūyù yánsè míngxiǎn biàn qiǎn le, ér jiājù zhēdǎng de qūyù zé bǎochí zhe yuánlái de shēn sèdiào. Rúguǒ nǐ bān kāi yí gè chángqī fàngzhì de guìzi, jiù néng kàn dào dìbǎn shàng qīngxī de yánsè fēnjiè xiàn — nà shì guāngxiàn rì fù yí rì 「shūxiě」 zài mùtou shàng de hénjì. Wǒ hái zhùyì dào, wǔhòu de yángguāng chuān guò chuānghù shàng de bùtóng qūyù huì chéngxiàn wēimiào de sèwēn chāyì: kàojìn chuāngkuàng biānyuán de guāngxiàn lüè dài nuǎnsè, yīnwèi jīngguò le chuāngkuàng biǎomiàn de fǎnshè; ér zhíjiē chuān guò bōli zhōngyāng de guāngxiàn zé piānxiàng lěng bái. Zhèxiē chāyì ròuyǎn jīhū chájué bú dào, dàn dìbǎn shàng chángnián jīlěi de sèchā zhōngshí de jìlù le měi yì tiáo guāngxiàn de wēndù hé guǐjì.
text_en: Every room has its own unique light 'fingerprint,' determined jointly by window orientation, area, obstructions, and interior materials. My living room faces south; around ten in the morning sunlight streams through the window, casting a bright rectangular patch on the wooden floor. This light patch moves slowly each day — at the summer solstice it reaches only one-third of the room, but at the winter solstice it extends to the foot of the opposite wall. Years of sunlight exposure have created varying color differences across different floor areas: regions frequently covered by sunlight have noticeably lightened, while areas blocked by furniture retain their original dark tone. If you move a long-standing cabinet, you can see a clear color boundary line on the floor — the trace of light 'writing' on wood day after day. I've also noticed that afternoon sunlight passing through different parts of the window shows subtle color temperature differences: light near the window frame edge carries a slightly warm tone, having reflected off the frame's surface; while light passing directly through the center of the glass leans toward cool white. These differences are nearly imperceptible to the naked eye, but the color variations accumulated on the floor over years faithfully record every ray of light's temperature and trajectory.

questions.q_en: How far does the sunlight patch extend at the winter solstice?

title: The Breathing of an Old House
text_zh: 住在老房子里久了，你会发现它其实在呼吸。每天清晨，当太阳开始照射东面的外墙时，木质的门框会发出细微的「嘎吱」声——那是木材在温度升高时膨胀的声响。到了傍晚温度下降，相同的门框又会发出一声几乎听不见的轻响，像是在缓缓地叹气。雨天的老房子呼吸得更加明显：墙壁吸收了空气中的水分以后会略微膨胀，门窗因此变得比平时更难推开；等到晴天空气变干，墙壁收缩，那些原本紧绷的门又恢复了正常的松弛。我曾经在一个特别潮湿的梅雨季节记录下整栋房子各处门窗的开合阻力变化，结果发现南面的窗户变化最大——因为南面接受的阳光和雨水都最为充沛，经历的膨胀收缩循环也最频繁。住了五年以后，我几乎可以根据门框的声音判断当天的温度变化幅度，根据推门的手感判断空气湿度的高低。老房子是一个巨大的温湿度传感器，它用自己的身体感知天气，然后用吱嘎声和阻力变化向你报告。
text_pinyin: Zhù zài lǎo fángzi lǐ jiǔ le, nǐ huì fāxiàn tā qíshí zài hūxī. Měi tiān qīngchén, dāng tàiyáng kāishǐ zhàoshè dōng miàn de wàiqiáng shí, mùzhì de ménkuàng huì fāchū xìwēi de 「gāzhī」 shēng — nà shì mùcái zài wēndù shēnggāo shí péngzhàng de shēngxiǎng. Dào le bàngwǎn wēndù xiàjiàng, xiāngtóng de ménkuàng yòu huì fāchū yì shēng jīhū tīng bú jiàn de qīng xiǎng, xiàng shì zài huǎnhuǎn de tànqì. Yǔ tiān de lǎo fángzi hūxī de gèngjiā míngxiǎn: qiángbì xīshōu le kōngqì zhōng de shuǐfèn yǐhòu huì lüèwēi péngzhàng, ménchuāng yīncǐ biàn de bǐ píngshí gèng nán tuī kāi; děng dào qíngtiān kōngqì biàn gān, qiángbì shōusuō, nàxiē yuánběn jǐnbēng de mén yòu huīfù le zhèngcháng de sōngchí. Wǒ céngjīng zài yí gè tèbié cháoshī de méiyǔ jìjié jìlù xià zhěng dòng fángzi gè chù ménchuāng de kāihé zǔlì biànhuà, jiéguǒ fāxiàn nánmiàn de chuānghù biànhuà zuìdà — yīnwèi nánmiàn jiēshòu de yángguāng hé yǔshuǐ dōu zuìwéi chōngpèi, jīnglì de péngzhàng shōusuō xúnhuán yě zuì pínfán. Zhù le wǔ nián yǐhòu, wǒ jīhū kěyǐ gēnjù ménkuàng de shēngyīn pànduàn dāngtiān de wēndù biànhuà fúdù, gēnjù tuī mén de shǒugǎn pànduàn kōngqì shīdù de gāodī. Lǎo fángzi shì yí gè jùdà de wēn shīdù chuángǎnqì, tā yòng zìjǐ de shēntǐ gǎnzhī tiānqì, ránhòu yòng zhīgā shēng hé zǔlì biànhuà xiàng nǐ bàogào.
text_en: Live in an old house long enough and you'll discover it actually breathes. Every morning, when the sun begins heating the east-facing exterior wall, the wooden door frame emits a faint creaking sound — the noise of wood expanding as temperature rises. By evening when temperatures drop, the same frame emits a barely audible sound, as if slowly sighing. An old house breathes more noticeably on rainy days: after the walls absorb moisture from the air they swell slightly, making doors and windows harder to push open than usual; when sunny weather returns and the air dries, the walls contract, and those formerly tight doors return to their normal looseness. During one particularly humid plum rain season, I recorded the changes in opening resistance of doors and windows throughout the house, finding the south-facing windows changed most — because the south side receives the most abundant sunlight and rain, experiencing the most frequent expansion-contraction cycles. After five years of living here, I can almost judge the day's temperature range from the door frame's sounds, and gauge air humidity from the feel of pushing a door. An old house is a giant temperature and humidity sensor, perceiving weather with its own body and reporting to you through creaks and resistance changes.

questions.q_zh: 雨天老房子的门窗有什么变化？
questions.q_en: How do the old house's doors and windows change on rainy days?

options.pinyin: biàn de bǐ píngshí gèng nán tuī kāi
options.text_en: Become harder to push open than usual

questions.explanation: On rainy days the old house's wooden frames absorb moisture and swell, making doors and windows noticeably harder to push open.

questions.q_zh: 作者选择这个视角有什么效果？
questions.q_en: What effect does the author's choice of perspective have?
questions.explanation: The first-person observational perspective creates sensory intimacy, drawing the reader into direct experience rather than maintaining analytical distance.

title: The Night Shift of a Convenience Store
text_zh: 凌晨两点的便利店有一种独特的宁静。白天人来人往的货架在夜里显得格外整齐，每一排商品都像是列队站好的士兵，等待着下一位顾客的检阅。夜班店员小刘正在补货，她把一箱箱饮料码放在冰柜里的动作轻柔而熟练，像是在图书馆里上架图书。每隔二十分钟左右，门口的感应铃会叮咚响一次——进来的人大多是出租车司机和刚下夜班的护士，他们的表情带着一种共同的倦怠感，但挑选食物的动作却出奇地认真。一个中年司机在饭团区域犹豫了整整三分钟，最后选了一个金枪鱼口味的。小刘告诉我，她能通过顾客选择的商品大致判断他们的职业和心情：买咖啡和能量饮料的多半还有工作要做，买啤酒和零食的通常是刚结束社交活动，买方便面和矿泉水的往往是深夜独自一人回家的年轻人。凌晨四点半左右是一天中最安静的时刻，小刘会趁这个间隙靠在收银台后面闭眼休息五分钟。窗外的天色刚刚开始发白，便利店的灯光在黎明前的街道上画出了一个温暖的长方形。
text_pinyin: Língchén liǎng diǎn de biànlìdiàn yǒu yì zhǒng dútè de níngjìng. Báitiān rén lái rén wǎng de huòjià zài yèlǐ xiǎn de géwài zhěngqí, měi yì pái shāngpǐn dōu xiàng shì lièduì zhàn hǎo de shìbīng, děngdài zhe xià yí wèi gùkè de jiǎnyuè. Yèbān diànyuán Xiǎo Liú zhèngzài bǔ huò, tā bǎ yì xiāng xiāng yǐnliào mǎ fàng zài bīngguì lǐ de dòngzuò qīngróu ér shúliàn, xiàng shì zài túshūguǎn lǐ shàng jià túshū. Měi gé èrshí fēnzhōng zuǒyòu, ménkǒu de gǎnyìng líng huì dīngdōng xiǎng yí cì — jìnlái de rén dàduō shì chūzūchē sījī hé gāng xià yèbān de hùshi, tāmen de biǎoqíng dài zhe yì zhǒng gòngtóng de juàndài gǎn, dàn tiāoxuǎn shíwù de dòngzuò què chūqí de rènzhēn. Yí gè zhōngnián sījī zài fàntuán qūyù yóuyù le zhěngzhěng sān fēnzhōng, zuìhòu xuǎn le yí gè jīnqiāngyú kǒuwèi de. Xiǎo Liú gàosu wǒ, tā néng tōngguò gùkè xuǎnzé de shāngpǐn dàzhì pànduàn tāmen de zhíyè hé xīnqíng: mǎi kāfēi hé néngliàng yǐnliào de duōbàn hái yǒu gōngzuò yào zuò, mǎi píjiǔ hé língshí de tōngcháng shì gāng jiéshù shèjiāo huódòng, mǎi fāngbiànmiàn hé kuàngquánshuǐ de wǎngwǎng shì shēnyè dúzì yì rén huí jiā de niánqīng rén. Língchén sì diǎn bàn zuǒyòu shì yì tiān zhōng zuì ānjìng de shíkè, Xiǎo Liú huì chèn zhège jiānxì kào zài shōuyíntái hòumiàn bì yǎn xiūxi wǔ fēnzhōng. Chuāng wài de tiānsè gānggāng kāishǐ fā bái, biànlìdiàn de dēngguāng zài límíng qián de jiēdào shàng huà chū le yí gè wēnnuǎn de chángfāngxíng.
text_en: A convenience store at two in the morning has a unique tranquility. The shelves bustling with shoppers during the day look exceptionally neat at night, each row of products standing at attention like soldiers awaiting the next customer's inspection. Night-shift clerk Xiao Liu is restocking, placing cases of drinks into the refrigerator with gentle, practiced movements, like shelving books at a library. About every twenty minutes the entrance sensor chimes — most who enter are taxi drivers and nurses just off the night shift, their expressions sharing a common weariness, yet they select food with surprising deliberation. A middle-aged driver hesitated in the rice ball section for a full three minutes before choosing a tuna flavor. Xiao Liu told me she can roughly judge customers' occupations and moods by what they buy: those getting coffee and energy drinks likely still have work to do; beer and snacks usually mean someone just finished socializing; instant noodles and mineral water typically mean a young person heading home alone late at night. Around 4:30 AM is the quietest moment of the day; Xiao Liu takes this lull to lean behind the cash register and rest her eyes for five minutes. Outside the window the sky has just begun to lighten, and the convenience store's light draws a warm rectangle on the pre-dawn street.

title: The Underground River of the Subway
text_zh: 乘坐地铁的时候，我总觉得自己是在一条地下河流中漂流。列车在隧道里穿行时产生的气流像是水流的涌动，到站开门的瞬间，一股气流从车厢内涌出，和站台上的空气混合在一起——那是两条「水流」汇合的时刻。乘客们就像河水中的漂浮物：有些人顺着人流的方向移动，毫不犹豫；有些人则在换乘通道的分叉口短暂停留，像是卡在石头缝隙里的枯叶，等待下一个「水流」把他们带向正确的方向。地铁线路图挂在墙上，线条交错纵横，像极了一张水系图——每条线路都是一条河流，每个换乘站都是一个汇水点。高峰时段的人流量就像汛期的河水，有时候会在某些瓶颈路段形成「堰塞」，人群被迫减速甚至停滞。我特别喜欢在非高峰时段乘地铁：车厢半空，窗外的隧道壁飞速后退，偶尔闪过一盏昏暗的检修灯，像河底深处偶尔闪烁的光点。到达终点站以后，所有乘客都下车了，空空的列车继续向前驶入车库——那是河流最终汇入的地下湖泊。
text_pinyin: Chéngzuò dìtiě de shíhou, wǒ zǒng juéde zìjǐ shì zài yì tiáo dìxià héliú zhōng piāoliú. Lièchē zài suìdào lǐ chuānxíng shí chǎnshēng de qìliú xiàng shì shuǐliú de yǒngdòng, dào zhàn kāi mén de shùnjiān, yì gǔ qìliú cóng chēxiāng nèi yǒng chū, hé zhàntái shàng de kōngqì hùnhé zài yìqǐ — nà shì liǎng tiáo 「shuǐliú」 huìhé de shíkè. Chéngkè men jiù xiàng héshuǐ zhōng de piāofúwù: yǒuxiē rén shùn zhe rénliú de fāngxiàng yídòng, háo bù yóuyù; yǒuxiē rén zé zài huànchéng tōngdào de fēnchàkǒu duǎnzàn tíngliú, xiàng shì kǎ zài shítou fèngxì lǐ de kūyè, děngdài xià yí gè 「shuǐliú」 bǎ tāmen dài xiàng zhèngquè de fāngxiàng. Dìtiě xiànlù tú guà zài qiáng shàng, xiàntiáo jiāocuò zònghéng, xiàng jí le yì zhāng shuǐxì tú — měi tiáo xiànlù dōu shì yì tiáo héliú, měi gè huànchéng zhàn dōu shì yí gè huì shuǐ diǎn. Gāofēng shíduàn de rénliúliàng jiù xiàng xùnqī de héshuǐ, yǒushíhou huì zài mǒuxiē píngjǐng lùduàn xíngchéng 「yànsè」, rénqún bèi pò jiǎnsù shènzhì tíngzhì. Wǒ tèbié xǐhuan zài fēi gāofēng shíduàn chéng dìtiě: chēxiāng bàn kōng, chuāng wài de suìdào bì fēisù hòutuì, ǒu'ěr shǎn guò yì zhǎn hūn'àn de jiǎnxiū dēng, xiàng hé dǐ shēnchù ǒu'ěr shǎnshuò de guāngdiǎn. Dàodá zhōngdiǎn zhàn yǐhòu, suǒyǒu chéngkè dōu xià chē le, kōngkōng de lièchē jìxù xiàng qián shǐ rù chēkù — nà shì héliú zuìzhōng huì rù de dìxià húpō.
text_en: When riding the subway, I always feel I'm drifting in an underground river. The airflow generated as the train moves through tunnels feels like water currents surging; the moment it arrives at a station and the doors open, a rush of air pours out from the carriage and mixes with the platform's air — the moment two 'streams' converge. Passengers are like floating objects in river water: some move along the flow without hesitation; others pause briefly at junctions in transfer corridors, like dead leaves caught in rock crevices, waiting for the next 'current' to carry them in the right direction. The subway map hangs on the wall, lines crisscrossing, looking exactly like a river system chart — each line is a river, each transfer station a confluence point. Rush-hour passenger volume is like flood-season water, sometimes forming 'dams' at certain bottleneck sections, crowds forced to slow or even stall. I especially enjoy riding the subway during off-peak hours: the carriage half-empty, tunnel walls speeding past the windows, occasionally a dim maintenance lamp flashing by, like light points flickering deep at the river bottom. After reaching the terminal station, all passengers disembark, and the empty train continues forward into the depot — the underground lake where the river finally flows to rest.

questions.q_zh: 作者选择这个视角有什么效果？
questions.q_en: What effect does the author's choice of perspective have?
questions.explanation: The first-person observational perspective creates sensory intimacy, drawing the reader into direct experience rather than maintaining analytical distance.

questions.q_zh: 从这段话可以推断作者的什么立场？
questions.q_en: What stance of the author can be inferred from this passage?

options.text: 欣赏生活中的简单之美
options.pinyin: xīnshǎng shēnghuó zhōng de jiǎndān zhī měi

questions.explanation: The author's unhurried attention to ordinary detail, and the warmth embedded in the descriptions, reveal appreciation for quiet, unspectacular beauty in everyday life.

title: The Rhythm of a Crosswalk
text_zh: 城市里的每个人行横道都有自己的节奏感。我家附近有一个信号灯周期为九十秒的十字路口：行人绿灯持续二十五秒，红灯六十五秒。在这二十五秒的窗口里，两边人行道上积蓄的人流同时涌入斑马线，形成两股相对而行的人流。有趣的是，这两股人流并不会正面碰撞，而是本能地各自偏向右侧，像两条交错而过的溪流。走在前排的人步速最快，他们通常在十五秒内就已经到达对面；中间的人保持匀速，不紧不慢；最后几秒钟冲过来的是那些刚好赶上绿灯尾巴的人，他们的步伐带着一种微微的紧张感。红灯亮起以后，人行道两端又开始缓慢地聚集等待的行人。起初只有零星几个人，然后逐渐增多，到红灯结束时已经积累了相当的「水压」。下一次绿灯亮起的瞬间，这股被压缩的人流再次释放，整个循环重新开始。我站在路口观察了一个小时，看着这个由信号灯指挥的人类呼吸——吸气、停顿、呼气——精确得像一个巨大的肺脏。
text_pinyin: Chéngshì lǐ de měi gè rénxíng héngdào dōu yǒu zìjǐ de jiézòu gǎn. Wǒ jiā fùjìn yǒu yí gè xìnhào dēng zhōuqī wéi jiǔshí miǎo de shízì lùkǒu: xíngrén lǜdēng chíxù èrshíwǔ miǎo, hóngdēng liùshíwǔ miǎo. Zài zhè èrshíwǔ miǎo de chuāngkǒu lǐ, liǎng biān rénxíngdào shàng jīxù de rénliú tóngshí yǒng rù bānmǎxiàn, xíngchéng liǎng gǔ xiāngduì ér xíng de rénliú. Yǒuqù de shì, zhè liǎng gǔ rénliú bìng bú huì zhèngmiàn pèngzhuàng, ér shì běnnéng de gèzì piān xiàng yòucè, xiàng liǎng tiáo jiāocuò ér guò de xīliú. Zǒu zài qián pái de rén bùsù zuì kuài, tāmen tōngcháng zài shíwǔ miǎo nèi jiù yǐjīng dàodá duìmiàn; zhōngjiān de rén bǎochí yúnsù, bù jǐn bú màn; zuìhòu jǐ miǎozhōng chōng guòlái de shì nàxiē gānghǎo gǎn shàng lǜdēng wěiba de rén, tāmen de bùfá dài zhe yì zhǒng wēiwēi de jǐnzhāng gǎn. Hóngdēng liàng qǐ yǐhòu, rénxíngdào liǎng duān yòu kāishǐ huǎnmàn de jùjí děngdài de xíngrén. Qǐchū zhǐ yǒu língxīng jǐ gè rén, ránhòu zhújiàn zēngduō, dào hóngdēng jiéshù shí yǐjīng jīlěi le xiāngdāng de 「shuǐyā」. Xià yí cì lǜdēng liàng qǐ de shùnjiān, zhè gǔ bèi yāsuō de rénliú zàicì shìfàng, zhěng gè xúnhuán chóngxīn kāishǐ. Wǒ zhàn zài lùkǒu guānchá le yí gè xiǎoshí, kàn zhe zhège yóu xìnhào dēng zhǐhuī de rénlèi hūxī — xīqì, tíngdùn, hūqì — jīngquè de xiàng yí gè jùdà de fèizàng.
text_en: Every crosswalk in the city has its own rhythm. Near my home there's an intersection with a ninety-second signal cycle: the pedestrian green lasts twenty-five seconds, the red sixty-five. During that twenty-five-second window, pedestrians accumulated on both sidewalks pour simultaneously onto the zebra crossing, forming two streams moving toward each other. Interestingly, these two flows don't collide head-on but instinctively drift to their own right, like two streams passing through each other. Those in front walk fastest, usually reaching the other side within fifteen seconds; people in the middle maintain a steady pace, neither rushed nor slow; the last few seconds bring those who barely caught the tail of the green light, their steps carrying a slight tension. After the red light comes on, pedestrians begin slowly gathering again at both ends of the crosswalk. At first just a few scattered people, then gradually increasing, building considerable 'water pressure' by the time the red ends. The instant the next green light turns on, this compressed flow is released again, and the whole cycle begins anew. I stood at the intersection observing for an hour, watching this human breathing conducted by traffic lights — inhale, pause, exhale — precise as a giant lung.

questions.q_en: How do the two opposing pedestrian flows avoid head-on collision?

title: The Archaeology of a Bus Stop
text_zh: 我每天等公交车的站台其实是一个小型的城市考古遗址。站牌下面的水泥地面经过多年的踩踏已经磨出了一条弧形的浅痕——那是等车的人在站牌前来回踱步留下的轨迹。弧形的中心正好对准站牌上的线路信息牌，说明大多数人等车时的站立位置与信息牌之间有一个最佳距离。站台的金属座椅已经坐凹了好几个位置，扶手上的漆被无数双手磨得露出了银色的金属底层。座椅旁边的垃圾桶周围地面颜色比其他区域深——那是长年累月的饮料渗漏和口香糖残渍形成的「污染晕」。最有历史感的是站台后面那堵水泥矮墙：上面层层叠叠贴着各种小广告的残留痕迹，新的覆盖旧的，像是城市版的地质沉积层。偶尔能在最底层的残片上辨认出十几年前的电话号码格式——那时候手机号码还是十一位数，但前面加了区号。一个下雨天，我在墙角发现了一枚嵌在水泥裂缝里的硬币，年份是二零零八年。它已经被泥土半掩，再过几年大概会被完全覆盖——一枚属于这个站台的微型化石。
text_pinyin: Wǒ měi tiān děng gōngjiāo chē de zhàntái qíshí shì yí gè xiǎoxíng de chéngshì kǎogǔ yízhǐ. Zhànpái xiàmiàn de shuǐní dìmiàn jīngguò duō nián de cǎità yǐjīng mó chū le yì tiáo húxíng de qiǎn hén — nà shì děng chē de rén zài zhànpái qián láihuí duó bù liú xià de guǐjì. Húxíng de zhōngxīn zhènghǎo duìzhǔn zhànpái shàng de xiànlù xìnxī pái, shuōmíng dà duōshù rén děng chē shí de zhànlì wèizhì yǔ xìnxī pái zhī jiān yǒu yí gè zuìjiā jùlí. Zhàntái de jīnshǔ zuòyǐ yǐjīng zuò āo le hǎo jǐ gè wèizhì, fúshǒu shàng de qī bèi wúshù shuāng shǒu mó de lùchū le yínsè de jīnshǔ dǐcéng. Zuòyǐ pángbiān de lājītǒng zhōuwéi dìmiàn yánsè bǐ qítā qūyù shēn — nà shì chángnián lěiyuè de yǐnliào shènlòu hé kǒuxiāngtáng cánzì xíngchéng de 「wūrǎn yūn」. Zuì yǒu lìshǐ gǎn de shì zhàntái hòumiàn nà dǔ shuǐní ǎi qiáng: shàngmiàn céngcéng diédié tiē zhe gè zhǒng xiǎo guǎnggào de cánliú hénjì, xīn de fùgài jiù de, xiàng shì chéngshì bǎn de dìzhì chénjī céng. Ǒu'ěr néng zài zuì dǐcéng de cánpiàn shàng biànrèn chū shí jǐ nián qián de diànhuà hàomǎ géshì — nà shíhou shǒujī hàomǎ hái shì shíyī wèi shù, dàn qiánmiàn jiā le qūhào. Yí gè xià yǔ tiān, wǒ zài qiáng jiǎo fāxiàn le yì méi qiàn zài shuǐní lièfèng lǐ de yìngbì, niánfèn shì èr líng líng bā nián. Tā yǐjīng bèi nítǔ bàn yǎn, zài guò jǐ nián dàgài huì bèi wánquán fùgài — yì méi shǔyú zhège zhàntái de wēixíng huàshí.
text_en: The bus stop where I wait daily is a small urban archaeological site. The cement ground has been worn into an arc — the trajectory of passengers pacing before the sign. The arc's center aligns with the route board, marking an optimal standing distance. The metal bench has been sat into depressions, armrest paint worn to bare metal by countless hands. The ground near the trash can is darker — a 'pollution halo' from years of beverage spills and gum. Most evocative is the low wall behind the stop: layers of remnant advertisement traces, new covering old, like geological sediment. You can make out phone number formats from over a decade ago on the deepest layers — when mobile numbers had area codes. On a rainy day, I found a 2008 coin wedged in a cement crack at the wall's base, half-covered by dirt. In a few years it'll be completely buried — a miniature fossil belonging to this bus stop.

title: The Invisible Borders of a Neighborhood
text_zh: 每个城市社区都有一些看不见的边界，不是画在地图上的行政区划线，而是居民们在日常生活中自然形成的活动范围界限。我花了几个月时间绘制自己社区的这些隐形边界。首先是「买菜边界」：社区北边的居民去北门外的早市，南边的居民去南门外的超市，中间有一条大约平行于中心路的分界线。两侧的居民几乎从不越过这条线去对面买菜，即使价格和品质可能更好。其次是「遛狗边界」：养小型犬的居民倾向于在小区花园内遛狗，养大型犬的居民则习惯走到小区外面的河边步道。两个群体的路线极少交叉，形成了两套平行的遛狗网络。最微妙的是「打招呼边界」——在电梯间和楼道里，同一层楼的邻居会点头微笑，隔一层楼的邻居偶尔寒暄几句，隔两层以上的通常视而不见。社交亲密度随着楼层距离精确递减，大约每隔一层楼就降低百分之五十。这些边界没有人刻意设定，也没有人明确意识到它们的存在，但它们像一张无形的网覆盖着整个社区，悄悄地组织着每个人的日常轨迹。
text_pinyin: Měi gè chéngshì shèqū dōu yǒu yìxiē kàn bú jiàn de biānjiè, bú shì huà zài dìtú shàng de xíngzhèng qūhuà xiàn, ér shì jūmín men zài rìcháng shēnghuó zhōng zìrán xíngchéng de huódòng fànwéi jièxiàn. Wǒ huā le jǐ gè yuè shíjiān huìzhì zìjǐ shèqū de zhèxiē yǐnxíng biānjiè. Shǒuxiān shì 「mǎi cài biānjiè」: shèqū běibiān de jūmín qù běi mén wài de zǎoshì, nánbiān de jūmín qù nán mén wài de chāoshì, zhōngjiān yǒu yì tiáo dàyuē píngxíng yú zhōngxīn lù de fēnjiè xiàn. Liǎng cè de jūmín jīhū cóng bù yuè guò zhè tiáo xiàn qù duìmiàn mǎi cài, jíshǐ jiàgé hé pǐnzhì kěnéng gèng hǎo. Qícì shì 「liú gǒu biānjiè」: yǎng xiǎoxíng quǎn de jūmín qīngxiàng yú zài xiǎoqū huāyuán nèi liú gǒu, yǎng dàxíng quǎn de jūmín zé xíguàn zǒu dào xiǎoqū wàimiàn de hé biān bùdào. Liǎng gè qúntǐ de lùxiàn jí shǎo jiāochā, xíngchéng le liǎng tào píngxíng de liú gǒu wǎngluò. Zuì wēimiào de shì 「dǎ zhāohu biānjiè」 — zài diàntī jiān hé lóudào lǐ, tóng yì céng lóu de línjū huì diǎntóu wēixiào, gé yì céng lóu de línjū ǒu'ěr hánxuān jǐ jù, gé liǎng céng yǐshàng de tōngcháng shì'érbújiàn. Shèjiāo qīnmìdù suí zhe lóucéng jùlí jīngquè dìjiǎn, dàyuē měi gé yì céng lóu jiù jiàngdī bǎi fēn zhī wǔshí. Zhèxiē biānjiè méiyǒu rén kèyì shèdìng, yě méiyǒu rén míngquè yìshí dào tāmen de cúnzài, dàn tāmen xiàng yì zhāng wúxíng de wǎng fùgài zhe zhěng gè shèqū, qiāoqiāo de zǔzhī zhe měi gè rén de rìcháng guǐjì.
text_en: Every neighborhood has invisible borders — not administrative lines on maps, but natural limits of activity formed through daily life. I spent months mapping these in my community. First, the 'grocery boundary': north-side residents go to the morning market outside the north gate, south-side residents go to the supermarket outside the south gate, divided roughly along the central road. Residents almost never cross this line, even if prices are better. Next, the 'dog-walking boundary': small-dog owners walk within the compound's garden; large-dog owners go to the riverside path outside. The two groups rarely cross, forming parallel networks. Most subtle is the 'greeting boundary': same-floor neighbors exchange nods, one-floor-apart neighbors occasionally chat, two-or-more floors apart usually ignore each other. Social intimacy drops roughly fifty percent per floor. Nobody deliberately sets these boundaries or is aware of them, but they cover the community like an invisible net, quietly organizing daily trajectories.

title: The Night Market After Rain
text_zh: 雨刚停的夜市有一种特别的美感。地面上的积水映射着各个摊位的灯光，形成了一个倒置的灯火世界——真实的灯笼在上方，镜像的灯笼在脚下，行人走在这两层灯光之间，像是悬浮在半空中。湿润的空气把各种食物的气味放大了：烤羊肉串的烟雾变得更浓郁，臭豆腐的气味传播得更远，炒栗子的焦糖香气则在潮湿的空气中凝结成一种近乎可以触摸的甜蜜。摊主们忙着用抹布擦干塑料凳子上的雨水，动作麻利又带着一点抱怨的表情——雨天生意总是差一些，但雨后的反弹往往特别热闹。我注意到雨后的顾客有一个共同特点：他们的购买决策比平时更快，似乎因为在室内等雨而积攒了一种需要释放的消费欲望。一位卖煎饼果子的阿姨告诉我，雨后半小时是她一天中最忙碌的时段——甚至比饭点还忙。夜市的尽头，几个孩子在积水坑里踩水玩耍，他们的笑声和摊主的叫卖声交织在一起，构成了这个城市最生动的夜间合唱。
text_pinyin: Yǔ gāng tíng de yèshì yǒu yì zhǒng tèbié de měigǎn. Dìmiàn shàng de jīshuǐ yìngshè zhe gè gè tānwèi de dēngguāng, xíngchéng le yí gè dàozhì de dēnghuǒ shìjiè — zhēnshí de dēnglong zài shàngfāng, jìngxiàng de dēnglong zài jiǎo xià, xíngrén zǒu zài zhè liǎng céng dēngguāng zhī jiān, xiàng shì xuánfú zài bànkōng zhōng. Shīrùn de kōngqì bǎ gè zhǒng shíwù de qìwèi fàngdà le: kǎo yángròu chuàn de yānwù biàn de gèng nóngyù, chòu dòufu de qìwèi chuánbō de gèng yuǎn, chǎo lìzi de jiāotáng xiāngqì zé zài cháoshī de kōngqì zhōng níngjié chéng yì zhǒng jìnhū kěyǐ chùmō de tiánmì. Tānzhǔ men máng zhe yòng mābù cā gān sùliào dèngzi shàng de yǔshuǐ, dòngzuò málì yòu dài zhe yìdiǎn bàoyuàn de biǎoqíng — yǔ tiān shēngyì zǒng shì chà yìxiē, dàn yǔ hòu de fǎntán wǎngwǎng tèbié rènào. Wǒ zhùyì dào yǔ hòu de gùkè yǒu yí gè gòngtóng tèdiǎn: tāmen de gòumǎi juécè bǐ píngshí gèng kuài, sìhū yīnwèi zài shìnèi děng yǔ ér jīzǎn le yì zhǒng xūyào shìfàng de xiāofèi yùwàng. Yí wèi mài jiānbing guǒzi de āyí gàosu wǒ, yǔ hòu bàn xiǎoshí shì tā yì tiān zhōng zuì mánglù de shíduàn — shènzhì bǐ fàndiǎn hái máng. Yèshì de jìntóu, jǐ gè háizi zài jīshuǐ kēng lǐ cǎi shuǐ wánshuǎ, tāmen de xiàoshēng hé tānzhǔ de jiàomài shēng jiāozhī zài yìqǐ, gòuchéng le zhège chéngshì zuì shēngdòng de yèjiān héchàng.
text_en: A night market just after rain has a special beauty. Puddles on the ground reflect each stall's lights, forming an inverted world of lanterns — real lanterns above, mirrored lanterns below, pedestrians walking between these two layers of light as if suspended in midair. The humid air amplifies every food aroma: the smoke from grilled lamb skewers becomes richer, the scent of stinky tofu travels farther, and the caramel fragrance of roasted chestnuts condenses in the moist air into a sweetness you can almost touch. Vendors busily wipe rain from plastic stools with rags, their movements brisk with a hint of complaint — business is always slower on rainy days, but the post-rain rebound is often especially lively. I notice post-rain customers share a common trait: their purchasing decisions are faster than usual, as if waiting indoors during the rain has built up a consumer desire that needs release. A woman selling jianbing guozi told me the half-hour after rain is her busiest period of the day — even busier than mealtimes. At the far end of the night market, several children splash in puddles, their laughter intertwining with vendors' hawking calls, composing this city's most vivid nighttime chorus.

title: The Windows of an Apartment Building
text_zh: 晚上散步回来的路上，我总是忍不住抬头看公寓楼的窗户。每一扇亮着灯的窗户都是一个独立的发光像素，合在一起构成了一幅巨大的、不断变化的马赛克画面。黄色的灯光最多，那是客厅或卧室里普通照明的温暖色调；偏白偏蓝的光通常来自电视或电脑屏幕，说明有人正在看节目或者加班；偶尔出现的紫色调光意味着某户人家安装了智能灯泡，正在享受「氛围照明」。我注意到，不同楼层的灯光熄灭有一个明显的时间梯度：低楼层的灯大约在十点半以后开始逐渐熄灭，可能住着有孩子的家庭；中间楼层坚持到十一点半左右；而顶楼的几户常常亮到凌晨一两点——那里住着年轻人的概率最高。有一扇窗户每天晚上十一点整准时亮起一盏小台灯，持续大约四十五分钟后熄灭。我猜那是一个有夜间阅读习惯的人。窗帘后面的人影偶尔晃动，但我永远不会知道他们在读什么书。这种不完整的了解恰恰是城市生活最迷人的地方——你被无数陌生的生活包围着，感受到它们的温度，却永远无法触及它们的内容。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshàng sànbù huílái de lù shàng, wǒ zǒng shì rěn bú zhù táitóu kàn gōngyù lóu de chuānghù. Měi yì shàn liàng zhe dēng de chuānghù dōu shì yí gè dúlì de fāguāng xiàngsù, hé zài yìqǐ gòuchéng le yì fú jùdà de, búduàn biànhuà de mǎsàikè huàmiàn. Huángsè de dēngguāng zuìduō, nà shì kètīng huò wòshì lǐ pǔtōng zhàomíng de wēnnuǎn sèdiào; piān bái piān lán de guāng tōngcháng láizì diànshì huò diànnǎo píngmù, shuōmíng yǒu rén zhèngzài kàn jiémù huòzhě jiābān; ǒu'ěr chūxiàn de zǐsè diào guāng yìwèi zhe mǒu hù rénjiā ānzhuāng le zhìnéng dēngpào, zhèngzài xiǎngshòu 「fēnwéi zhàomíng」. Wǒ zhùyì dào, bùtóng lóucéng de dēngguāng xīmiè yǒu yí gè míngxiǎn de shíjiān tīdù: dī lóucéng de dēng dàyuē zài shí diǎn bàn yǐhòu kāishǐ zhújiàn xīmiè, kěnéng zhù zhe yǒu háizi de jiātíng; zhōngjiān lóucéng jiānchí dào shíyī diǎn bàn zuǒyòu; ér dǐng lóu de jǐ hù chángcháng liàng dào língchén yī liǎng diǎn — nàlǐ zhù zhe niánqīng rén de gàilǜ zuì gāo. Yǒu yì shàn chuānghù měi tiān wǎnshàng shíyī diǎn zhěng zhǔnshí liàng qǐ yì zhǎn xiǎo táidēng, chíxù dàyuē sìshíwǔ fēnzhōng hòu xīmiè. Wǒ cāi nà shì yí gè yǒu yèjiān yuèdú xíguàn de rén. Chuānglián hòumiàn de rényǐng ǒu'ěr huǎngdòng, dàn wǒ yǒngyuǎn bú huì zhīdào tāmen zài dú shénme shū. Zhè zhǒng bù wánzhěng de liǎojiě qiàqià shì chéngshì shēnghuó zuì mírén de dìfāng — nǐ bèi wúshù mòshēng de shēnghuó bāowéi zhe, gǎnshòu dào tāmen de wēndù, què yǒngyuǎn wúfǎ chùjí tāmen de nèiróng.
text_en: On my walk home in the evening, I can't help looking up at the apartment building's windows. Each lit window is an independent glowing pixel; together they compose a vast, constantly changing mosaic. Yellow light is most common — the warm tone of ordinary lighting in living rooms or bedrooms; whitish-blue light usually comes from TV or computer screens, indicating someone is watching a show or working late; occasional purple-toned light means a household has installed smart bulbs and is enjoying 'ambient lighting.' I notice that lights going off on different floors follow a clear time gradient: lower floor lights begin gradually turning off after about 10:30, likely families with children; middle floors hold out until around 11:30; while several units on the top floor often stay lit until one or two in the morning — the highest probability of young residents. One window lights up a small desk lamp at exactly 11 PM every night, lasting about forty-five minutes before going dark. I guess it's someone with a nighttime reading habit. Silhouettes behind curtains occasionally shift, but I'll never know what book they're reading. This incomplete understanding is precisely the most enchanting aspect of city life — you're surrounded by countless unfamiliar lives, feeling their warmth, yet never able to touch their content.

questions.q_zh: 偏白偏蓝的窗户灯光通常来自什么？
questions.q_en: What do whitish-blue window lights usually come from?
questions.explanation: The passage evidence ('whitish-blue light usually comes from TV or computer screens,...') supports this answer; alternatives either contradict stated details or lack textual basis.

questions.q_zh: 如果另一个人来到同样的场景，会有同样的感受吗？
questions.q_en: Would another person in the same setting have the same feelings?

options.text: 不一定，取决于个人经历
options.pinyin: bù yídìng, qǔjué yú gèrén jīnglì
options.text_en: not necessarily, it depends on personal experience
